Table NameEquity_instrument_that_is_not_a_security_as_a_hedge
Functional NameEquity instrument that is not a security as a hedge
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionLoan and advance as a hedge is a Loan and advance role where the Loan and advance instrument acts as a non-derivative hedge.
Notes
Number Of Columns7
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Accounting_standardPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
5Instrument_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Accounting_hedge_indicatorYString (1) DOMAccounting hedge indicator
7Type_of_hedgeYString (2) DOMType of hedge
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Instrument_identifierAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
6Accounting_hedge_indicatorAn indicator specifying if the hedge is considered an accounting hedge or not.
7Type_of_hedgeType of hedge applicable under the relevant accounting standard.

Values List
ValueDescription
1Fair value hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (a)
11Hedges other than cash flow hedge and hedge of net investment in a foreign operation: Economic hedge
12Economic hedge. With use of fair value option
13Economic hedge. Other
2Cash flow hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (b)
3Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (c)
4Portfolio fair value hedges of interest rate risk: Hedging relationship as defined in IAS39.AG 114-132
5Portfolio cash flow hedges of interest rate risk: Hedging relationship as defined in IAS39.AG 114-132
6Cost-price hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.114. This hedging relationship is applicable only under nGAAP based on BAD
7Other than Fair value hedge, Cash flow hedge, Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation, Portfolio fair value hedges of interest rate risk, Portfolio cash flow hedges of interest rate risk, Cost-price hedge: Other applicable hedging relationship for nGAAP based on BAD reporters

Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999

Expected Growth0

NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation

NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes

Functional NameExchange tradable derivative position
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionAn Exchange tradable derivative position is a Security or Exchange tradable derivative position with an Exchange tradable derivative.
Notes
Number Of Columns12
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
2Exchange_tradable_derivative_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
4Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
6Buyer_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
7Seller_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
8Buyer_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
9Seller_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
10Notional_amountYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
11Security_or_Exchange_tradable_derivative_position_typeYString (1) DOMSecurity or Exchange tradable derivative position type (Input Layer)
12Held_for_sale_indicatorYString (1) DOMHeld for sale indicator (Input Layer)
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
2Exchange_tradable_derivative_identifierThe security identifier of the exchange tradable derivative.
3Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
4Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
6Buyer_role_typeThe buyer party role.
7Seller_role_typeThe seller role.
8Buyer_identifierThe buyer party role.
9Seller_identifierThe seller role.
10Notional_amountThe notional amount on a financial instrument is the nominal or face amount that is used to calculate payments made on that instrument.
11Security_or_Exchange_tradable_derivative_position_typeA discriminator to distinguish between security positions and tradable derivative positions.
12Held_for_sale_indicatorHeld for sale  indicates the Non-current assets and instruments in disposal groups classified as held for sale in accordance with IFRS5. Is not applicable under national GAAP based on BAD.

7Clearing member
8Issuer

Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Held for sale: The member indicates that the instrument is held for sale
2Not held for sale: The member indicates that the instrument is not held for sale

Table NameExchange_tradable_derivative_position_Protection_arrangement_assignment
Functional NameExchange tradable derivative position Protection arrangement assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionExchange tradable derivative position Protection arrangement assignment is the combination of Exchange tradable derivative position and Protection arrangement   stating which Exchange tradable derivative position is offering protection for which Protection arrangement.
Notes
Number Of Columns14
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Protection_arrangement_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2Protection_arrangement_role_typePFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
4Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
6Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
7Exchange_tradable_derivative_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8Buyer_role_typeFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
9Seller_role_typeFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
10Buyer_identifierFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
11Seller_identifierFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
12Allocated_unused_collateral_valueYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
13Exchange_tradable_derivative_Protection_arrangement_assignment_typeYString (1) DOMExchange tradable derivative Protection arrangement assignment type
14Protection_allocated_valueYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Protection_arrangement_identifierAn identifier to univocally identify each protection arragement.
2Protection_arrangement_role_typeProtection arrangement role type distinguishes the Protection arrangement role according to their nature.
3Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
4Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
5Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
7Exchange_tradable_derivative_identifierThe security identifier of the exchange tradable derivative.
12Allocated_unused_collateral_valueAllocated unused collateral value is an amount that expresses the protection value that is not used for risk mitigation and, for FINREP reporting purposes is allocated to a specific Loan and advance, which is the Long non-negotiable security position.
13Exchange_tradable_derivative_Protection_arrangement_assignment_typeExchange tradable derivative Protection arrangement assignment type distinguishes Exchange tradable derivative Protection arrangement assignment according to the direction, given or received.
14Protection_allocated_valueProtection allocated value is the maximum amount of the protection value, via the Protection arrangement, that can be considered as credit protection for the Long security position. For protections that is eligible under Regulation (EU) No 575/2013, this value should be reported in accordance with Part 2 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
Indexes
Index NameStateFunctionalSpatialExpressionColumn NameSort

Table NameExchange_tradable_derivative_position_role
Functional NameBalance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative asset position derived data
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionAn Exchange tradable derivative position role is an Exchange tradable derivative position acting in a specific role.
Notes
Number Of Columns32
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Seller_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2Seller_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
3Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
4Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5Buyer_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Buyer_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
7Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
8Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
9Exchange_tradable_derivative_position_role_typePYString (2) DOMExchange tradable derivative position role type (Input Layer)
10Exchange_tradable_derivative_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
11Accounting_classificationYString (3) DOMAccounting classification (Input Layer)
12Asset_encumbrance_source_indicatorYStringDOMAsset encumbrance source indicator (Input Layer)
13At_cost-based_method_or_Lower_of_costs_or_market_(LOCOM)_indicatorYString (1) DOMHedge accounting - At cost based method or Lower of cost or market (LOCOM) indicator (Input layer)
14Encumbered_asset_indicatorYStringDOMEncumbered asset indicator (Input Layer)
15Fair_value_hierarchyYString (1) DOMFair value hierarchy (Input Layer)
16Fiduciary_asset_indicatorYString (1) DOMFiduciary asset indicator (Input layer)
17Prudential_portfolio_typeYString (1) DOMPrudential portfolio type (Input Layer)
18Type_of_hedgeYString (2) DOMType of hedge (Input layer)
19Time_since_initial_recognitionYStringDOMTime elapsed since initial recognition band (Input Layer)
20Balance_sheet_recognised_exchange_tradable_derivative_asset_position_accounting_standard_typeYString (2) DOMBalance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative asset position by accounting standard (Input layer)
21Balance_sheet_recognised_exchange_tradable_derivative_asset_position_taken_into_possession_typeYString (2) DOMBalance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative asset position taken into possession type (Input Layer)
22Accumulated_changes_in_fair_valueDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
23Accumulated_fair_value_changes_of_the_exposure_due_to_credit_riskDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
24Balance_sheet_recognition_dateDateDOMAll possible dates
25Carrying_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
26Derecognised_exposure_accumulated_impairmentDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
27Derecognised_exposure_gross_carrying_amountDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
28Fair_valueDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
29Fair_value_changeDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
30Gross_carrying_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
31Gross_carrying_amount_at_initial_recognitionDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
32Accumulated_negative_changesDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Seller_identifierThe party identifier of the involved seller.
2Seller_role_typeThe seller role.
3Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
4Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
5Buyer_identifierThe party identifier of the involved buyer.
6Buyer_role_typeThe buyer party role.
7Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
11Accounting_classificationAccounting portfolio where the asset or liability is recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP –under Regulation (EU) 2015/534 (ECB/2015/13) applied by the observed agent's legal entity.
12Asset_encumbrance_source_indicatorAsset encumbrance source indicator signals whether the liabity or off-balance sheet item is a source of encumbrance for an asset or not.
13At_cost-based_method_or_Lower_of_costs_or_market_(LOCOM)_indicatorAt cost based method or Lower of costs or market (LOCOM) indicator distinguishes the used accounting portfolio for national General Accepted Accounting Principles (nGAAP) between those that use the At cost based method or Lower of costs or market (LOCOM), and those that do not use At cost based method or Lower of costs or carket (LOCOM).
14Encumbered_asset_indicatorEncumbered asset indicator signals whether the asset is encumbered or not.
15Fair_value_hierarchyLevel of fair value of a financial asset or liability according to the hierarchy defined in IFRS 13. Applicable to national GAAP based on BAD only if the allocation of instruments is measured at fair value according to different levels.
16Fiduciary_asset_indicatorIdentification of instruments in which the observed agent acts in its own name but on behalf of and with the risk borne by a third party.
17Prudential_portfolio_typeA discriminator to distinguish between trading and banking (or non-trading) book.
18Type_of_hedgeType of hedge applicable under the relevant accounting standard.
19Time_since_initial_recognitionTime elapsed since initial recognition band classifies time elapsed since initial recognition in the balance sheet into bands used for FINREP reporting.
20Balance_sheet_recognised_exchange_tradable_derivative_asset_position_accounting_standard_typeBalance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative asset position by accounting standard distinguishes the Balance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative asset position by the applied accounting standard.
21Balance_sheet_recognised_exchange_tradable_derivative_asset_position_taken_into_possession_typeBalance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative asset position obtained by taking possession type is a dsicriminant that distinguishes between Balance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative asset position obtained by taking possession and not obtained by taking possession
22Accumulated_changes_in_fair_valueAmount of gains and losses from re-measurement of the asset accumulated from the initial recognition to the reference date.
23Accumulated_fair_value_changes_of_the_exposure_due_to_credit_riskAccumulated changes in fair value due to credit risk in accordance with Part 2.46 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014 of the exposure that is derecognised because of the asset obtained by taking possession.

Note: This amount must only be reported when it is negative.
24Balance_sheet_recognition_dateBalance sheet recognition date is a date on the asset obtained by taking possession  was taken into possession and thus recognised on the balance sheet.
25Carrying_amountThe carrying amount in accordance with Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
26Derecognised_exposure_accumulated_impairmentAccumulated impairment is the amount of loss allowances that are held against or are allocated to the derecognised exposure on the reporting reference date for which the asset obtained by taking possession has been obtained.

This data attribute applies to assets subject to impairment under the applied accounting standard. Under IFRS, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected
credit losses;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected
credit losses.
Under GAAP, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to general allowances;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to specific allowances.
28Fair_valueFair value as defined in IFRS 13.9.
29Fair_value_changeFair value changes in the period and before taxes.
30Gross_carrying_amountGross carrying amount as defined in Annex V 5.2.
31Gross_carrying_amount_at_initial_recognitionGross carrying amount at the point in time of the initial recognition in the balance sheet 
32Accumulated_negative_changesAccumulated negative changes shall be the difference, at the level of the individual asset obtained by taking in to possession  item, between the value at initial recognition of the asset  and the carrying amount at the reporting reference date, where that difference is negative. (FINREP Annex V)

Values List

0Not applicable
1Solo consolidation level

Accounting_standard
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1National GAAP not consistent with IFRS
2IFRS
3National GAAP consistent with IFRS
Exchange_tradable_derivative_position_role_type
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
10Non-fair-valued balance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative asset position
12Non-balance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative asset position
41Exchange tradable derivative position as a hedge according to International Financial Reporting Standard (IFRS)
42Exchange tradable derivative position as a hedge according to national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP)
6Non-balance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative liability position
7Fair-valued balance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative liability position
8Non-fair-valued balance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative liability position
9Fair-valued balance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative asset position
Accounting_classification
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
10 nGAAP: Loans and receivables
12nGAAP: Held-to-maturity investments
14IFRS: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits in accordance with IFRS.
2IFRS: Financial assets held for trading: Financial assets held for trading in accordance with IFRS.
21IFRS: Financial liabilities measured at amortised cost: Financial liabilities measured at amortised cost in accordance with IFRS
23IFRS: Financial liabilities held for trading: Financial liabilities held for trading in accordance with IFRS
25IFRS: Financial liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with IFRS
3nGAAP: Trading Financial assets: Trading financial assets in accordance with national GAAP.
31nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial liabilities measured at a cost-based method: Non-trading non-derivative financial liabilities measured at a cost-based method accordance with national GAAP based on BAD
33nGAAP: Trading financial liabilities: Trading financial liabilities in accordance with national GAAP based on BAD
35nGAAP: Financial liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with nationa GAAP based on BAD
4IFRS: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial assets measured at fair value through profit and loss and designated as such upon initial recognition or subsequently in accordance with IFRS, except those classified as financial assets held for trading.
41IFRS: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with IFRS.
45nGAAP: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits in accordance with national GAAP.
46nGAAP: Financial assets held for trading
47nGAAP: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with national GAAP.
48nGAAP: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss
5nGAAP: Available-for-sale financial assets
6IFRS: Financial assets at amortised cost: Financial assets measured at amortised cost in accordance with IFRS.
64nGAAP: financial assets at fair value or strict LOCOM
7nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value through profit or loss: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity in accordance with national GAAP.
711Accounting portfolios for financial assets other than classified as held for sale, excluding financial assets held for trading, trading financial assets and cash and cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits
73nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets. LOCOM: nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets at LOCOM
74nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets. Other than LOCOM
76nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method. LOCOM: nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method at LOCOM
77nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method. Other than LOCOM
8IFRS: Financial assets at fair value through other comprehensive income: Financial assets measured at fair value through other comprehensive income due to business model and cash-flows characteristics in accordance with IFRS.
83Investments in subsidiaries, joint ventures and associates.
851nGAAP: Accounting portfolios for trading financial assetss. Cost based method or LOCOM
852nGAAP: Accounting portfolios for trading financial liabilities. Cost based method or LOCOM
9nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity in accordance with national GAAP.
90Under IFRS 9 impairment: Off balance sheet accounting classification: under IFRS 9 impairment
911Measured under IAS 37: Off balance sheet accounting classification: measured under IAS 37
912Measured under IFRS 4: Off balance sheet accounting classification: measured under IFRS 4
92Measured at fair value through profit or loss: Off balance sheet accounting classification: IFRS 9 fair valued commitments and financial guarantees
93Under nGAAP: Off balance sheet accounting classification: measured under nGAAP based on BAD
Asset_encumbrance_source_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Asset encumbrance source
2Not an asset encumbrance source
At_cost-based_method_or_Lower_of_costs_or_market_(LOCOM)_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1At cost based method or Lower of cost or market (LOCOM)
2Not at cost based method or Lower of cost or market(LOCOM)
Encumbered_asset_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Not encumbered asset
100Encumbered asset
Fair_value_hierarchy
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Level 1: Level 1 inputs used for the measurement of fair value. Level 1 inputs are quoted prices in active markets for identical assets or liabilities that the entity can access at the measurement date
2Level 2: Level 2 inputs used for the measurment of fair value. Level 2 inputs are inputs other than quoted prices included within Level 1 that are observable for the asset or liability, either directly or indirectly
3Level 3: Level 3 inputs used for the measurment of fair value. Level 3 inputs are unobservable inputs for the asset or liability
Fiduciary_asset_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Fiduciary asset
2Non-fiduciary asset
Prudential_portfolio_type
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Trading book
2Non-trading book
Type_of_hedge
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Fair value hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (a)
11Hedges other than cash flow hedge and hedge of net investment in a foreign operation: Economic hedge
12Economic hedge. With use of fair value option
13Economic hedge. Other
2Cash flow hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (b)
3Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (c)
4Portfolio fair value hedges of interest rate risk: Hedging relationship as defined in IAS39.AG 114-132
5Portfolio cash flow hedges of interest rate risk: Hedging relationship as defined in IAS39.AG 114-132
6Cost-price hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.114. This hedging relationship is applicable only under nGAAP based on BAD
7Other than Fair value hedge, Cash flow hedge, Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation, Portfolio fair value hedges of interest rate risk, Portfolio cash flow hedges of interest rate risk, Cost-price hedge: Other applicable hedging relationship for nGAAP based on BAD reporters

27<= 2 years

Table NameFinancial_contract
Functional NameFinancial contract
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Financial contract is a legally binding document under which one or multiple Instruments and / or Credit facilities are created.
Notes
Number Of Columns9
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
4Accounting_standardPYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
5Financial_contract_identifierPYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Syndicated_contract_identifierFString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Financial_contract_inception_dateYDateDOMAll possible dates
8Financial_contract_typeYString (1) DOMFinancial contract type
9Legal_final_maturity_dateDateDOMAll possible dates
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Financial_contract_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each financial contract.
6Syndicated_contract_identifier 'Contract identifier' applied by the lead arranger of the syndicated contract to uniquely identify each contract. Each syndicated contract will have one 'syndicated contract identifier'. This value will not change over time and cannot be used by the lead arranger as the contract identifier for any other contract. All creditors participating in the syndicated contract must use the same 'syndicated contract identifier'.
7Financial_contract_inception_dateThe date on which the current contractual relationship originated, i.e. the date on which the contract agreement became binding for all parties.
8Financial_contract_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different types of Financial contracts.
9Legal_final_maturity_dateThe contractual maturity date of the asset or liability, taking into account any agreements amending initial contracts.
Indexes
Index NameStateFunctionalSpatialExpressionColumn NameSort
Order

Reporting_agent_identifierASC
Accounting_consolidation_levelASC
Accounting_standardASC
Financial_contract_identifierASC

Column LevelAccounting_consolidation_level
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Solo consolidation level
2Group consolidation level

Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1National GAAP not consistent with IFRS
2IFRS
3National GAAP consistent with IFRS

Values List
ValueDescription
1Single financial contract
2Syndicated financial contract member

Table NameFinancial_guarantee_instrument_covering_a_Debt_security_Debt_security_assignment
Functional NameFinancial guarantee instrument covering a Debt security Debt security assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Financial guarantee instrument covering a Debt security Debt security assignment is the combination of a Financial guarantee covering a Debt security and a Debt security that indicates which Financial guarantee covering a Debt security is assigned to which Debt security.
Notes
Number Of Columns7
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Debt_security_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
3Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
4Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5Accounting_standardPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
6Instrument_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Protection_allocated_valueYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Debt_security_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
2Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
3Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
4Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
6Instrument_identifierAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
7Protection_allocated_valueThe maximum amount of the protection value that can be considered as credit protection for the instrument. The amount of the existing third parties or observed agent priority claims against the protection must be excluded in the protection allocated value. For protections that is eligible under Regulation (EU) No 575/2013, this value should be reported in accordance with Part 2 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.

Table NameFundamental_review_of_the_trading_book_standard_approach_risk_measure_for_ETD_positions
Functional NameFundamental review of the trading book standard approach risk measure for ETD positions
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA measured risk of an ETD position against a particular risk factor
Notes
Number Of Columns22
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Fundamental_review_of_the_trading_book_standard_approach_risk_measure_IDPYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
3Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
5Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
6Exchange_tradable_derivative_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Buyer_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
8Seller_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
9Buyer_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
10Seller_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
11Risk_factor_for_standardised_approach_identifierFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
12Currency_of_recordYString (3) DOMSubdomain for domain ISO-4217 Currency Codes (ISO4217 Domain) (Input Layer)
13Risk_measure_typeYString (1) DOMRisk measure type
14Risk_measure_type_by_positionYString (1) DOMRisk measure type by position
15Shock_directionYString (1) DOMShock direction
16Curvature_risk_weightDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
17Curvature_valuation_differenceDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
18Delta_sensitivityDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
19Option_maturityDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
20TenorDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
21Underlying_residual_maturityDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
22Vega_sensitivityDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
2Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
3Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
5Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
7Buyer_role_typeThe buyer party role.
8Seller_role_typeThe seller role.
9Buyer_identifierThe party identifier of the involved buyer.
10Seller_identifierThe party identifier of the involved seller.
11Risk_factor_for_standardised_approach_identifierRisk factor identifier is a number that uniquely identifies the Risk factor.
12Currency_of_recordCurrency of record is the currency in which the curvature risk is measured.
13Risk_measure_typeRisk measure type distinguishes Fundamental review of the trading book standard approach risk measure according to its nature.
14Risk_measure_type_by_positionA discriminator to distinguish between different types of risk measures by position.
15Shock_directionThe direction of the change in uderlying (up or down), for each non-linear position we will have an upshift and a downshift measure recorded.
16Curvature_risk_weightThe weight associated with the curvature.
17Curvature_valuation_differenceCurvature recorded as a difference between the normal valuation and  a revaluation after a small  increase OR decrease in an underyling risk factor, this is an amount interprted in the curency of record, e.g. 100 dollars. We only use carvature for non-linear instruments (e.g. options).
18Delta_sensitivityAn amount interpreted in the currency of the record, e.g., 100 dollars.
19Option_maturityThe option maturity to which this sensitivity relates.
20TenorThe tenor point of the related curve if this is a risk to a curve based risk factor.
21Underlying_residual_maturityWhere required, the underlying residual maturity to which the sensitivity is wwa created. Required when we consider that the sensitivity came from a change in a volatility surface.
22Vega_sensitivityThe sensitivity, as an amount (e.g. 100 dollars).
Indexes

Table NameFundamental_review_of_the_trading_book_standard_approach_risk_measure_for_OTC_positions
Functional NameFundamental review of the trading book standard approach risk measure for OTC positions
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA measured risk of an OTC position against a particular risk factor
Notes
Number Of Columns18
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Fundamental_review_of_the_trading_book_standard_approach_risk_measure_IDPYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
3Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
4Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5Accounting_standardPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
6Instrument_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Risk_factor_for_standardised_approach_identifierFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8Risk_measure_typeYString (1) DOMRisk measure type
9Risk_measure_type_by_positionYString (1) DOMRisk measure type by position
10Currency_of_recordYString (3) DOMSubdomain for domain ISO-4217 Currency Codes (ISO4217 Domain) (Input Layer)
11Shock_directionYString (1) DOMShock direction
12Curvature_risk_weightDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
13Curvature_valuation_differenceDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
14Delta_sensitivityDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
15Option_maturityDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
16TenorDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
17Underlying_residual_maturityDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
18Vega_sensitivityDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
2Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
3Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
4Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
6Instrument_identifierAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
7Risk_factor_for_standardised_approach_identifierRisk factor identifier is a number that uniquely identifies the Risk factor.
8Risk_measure_typeRisk measure type distinguishes Fundamental review of the trading book standard approach risk measure according to its nature.
9Risk_measure_type_by_positionA discriminator to distinguish between different types of risk measures by position.
10Currency_of_recordCurrency of record is the currency in which the curvature risk is measured.
11Shock_directionThe direction of the change in uderlying (up or down), for each non-linear position we will have an upshift and a downshift measure recorded.
12Curvature_risk_weightThe weight associated with the curvature.
13Curvature_valuation_differenceCurvature recorded as a difference between the normal valuation and  a revaluation after a small  increase OR decrease in an underyling risk factor, this is an amount interprted in the curency of record, e.g. 100 dollars. We only use carvature for non-linear instruments (e.g. options).
14Delta_sensitivityAn amount interpreted in the currency of the record, e.g., 100 dollars.
15Option_maturityThe option maturity to which this sensitivity relates.
16TenorThe tenor point of the related curve if this is a risk to a curve based risk factor.
17Underlying_residual_maturityWhere required, the underlying residual maturity to which the sensitivity is wwa created. Required when we consider that the sensitivity came from a change in a volatility surface.
18Vega_sensitivityThe sensitivity, as an amount (e.g. 100 dollars).
Indexes
Index NameStateFunctionalSpatialExpressionColumn NameSort

Table NameFundamental_review_of_the_trading_book_standard_approach_risk_measure_for_security_positions
Functional NameFundamental review of the trading book standard approach risk measure for security positions
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA measured risk of a Security position against a particular risk factor
Notes
Number Of Columns20
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Fundamental_review_of_the_trading_book_standard_approach_risk_measure_IDPYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
3Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
5Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
6Security_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Investor_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8Investor_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
9Risk_factor_for_standardised_approach_identifierFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
10Risk_measure_typeYString (1) DOMRisk measure type
11Risk_measure_type_by_positionYString (1) DOMRisk measure type by position
12Currency_of_recordYString (3) DOMSubdomain for domain ISO-4217 Currency Codes (ISO4217 Domain) (Input Layer)
13Shock_directionYString (1) DOMShock direction
14Curvature_risk_weightDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
15Curvature_valuation_differenceDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
16Delta_sensitivityDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
17Option_maturityDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
18TenorDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
19Underlying_residual_maturityDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
20Vega_sensitivityDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
2Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
3Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
4Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
6Security_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
7Investor_identifierAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
8Investor_role_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
9Risk_factor_for_standardised_approach_identifierRisk factor identifier is a number that uniquely identifies the Risk factor.
10Risk_measure_typeRisk measure type distinguishes Fundamental review of the trading book standard approach risk measure according to its nature.
11Risk_measure_type_by_positionA discriminator to distinguish between different types of risk measures by position.
12Currency_of_recordCurrency of record is the currency in which the curvature risk is measured.
13Shock_directionThe direction of the change in uderlying (up or down), for each non-linear position we will have an upshift and a downshift measure recorded.
14Curvature_risk_weightThe weight associated with the curvature.
15Curvature_valuation_differenceCurvature recorded as a difference between the normal valuation and  a revaluation after a small  increase OR decrease in an underyling risk factor, this is an amount interprted in the curency of record, e.g. 100 dollars. We only use carvature for non-linear instruments (e.g. options).
16Delta_sensitivityAn amount interpreted in the currency of the record, e.g., 100 dollars.
17Option_maturityThe option maturity to which this sensitivity relates.
18TenorThe tenor point of the related curve if this is a risk to a curve based risk factor.
19Underlying_residual_maturityWhere required, the underlying residual maturity to which the sensitivity is wwa created. Required when we consider that the sensitivity came from a change in a volatility surface.
20Vega_sensitivityThe sensitivity, as an amount (e.g. 100 dollars).

NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes

Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionGroup - Key management personnel assignment is the generalisation of the relationship types between any Group and any Key management personnel.

Number Of Rows Min.0

Expected Growth0

Table NameImmediate_parent_enterprise_assignment
Functional NameImmediate parent enterprise assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionImmediate parent assignment is the combination of a Central bank and private sector company and an Immediate parent that indicates which Central bank and private sector company has the role of immediate parent for which Central bank and private sector company.
Notes
Number Of Columns8
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Immediate_parent_central_bank_and_private_sector_company_party_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
3Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
5Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
6Immediate_parent_enterprise_Party_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
7Immediate_parent_enterprise_according_to_AnaCredit_indicatorYString (1) DOMImmediate parent according to AnaCredit indicator
8Percentage_of_ownershipDECIMALDOMReal numbers from 0 to 1 with 6 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Immediate_parent_central_bank_and_private_sector_company_party_identifierThe party identifier of the involved central bank and private sector company.
2Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
3Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
4Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
6Immediate_parent_enterprise_Party_role_typeImmediate parent enterprise Party role type is the role type identifying the central bank and private sector company role type as Immediate parent enterprise.
7Immediate_parent_enterprise_according_to_AnaCredit_indicatorAn indicator specifying if the immediate parent is the immediate parent enterprise according to Regulation (EU) 867/2016, data attribute Immediate parent undertaking identifier; "Counterparty identifier for the legal entity which is the immediate parent undertaking of the counterparty. If the counterparty has no parent undertaking, the counterparty identifier for the counterparty itself is to be reported. Parent undertaking has the same meaning as defined in Article 4(1)(15)(a) of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.".
8Percentage_of_ownershipThe percentage of ownership of the immediate parent as expressed in the Immediate parent assignment.

Table NameInstrument
Functional NameFactoring cash reserve
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionAn Instrument is a product that establishes a financial relationship between a bank and its customer.
In the context of accounting such a financial relationship may be considered a financial asset or a financial liabilty or an off-balance sheet item.
Notes
Number Of Columns38
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Accounting_standardPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
5Instrument_identifierPYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Credit_facility_identifierFString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Balance_sheet_netting_identifierFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8Financial_contract_identifierFString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
9(Extremely)_high_liquidity_and_credit_quality_indicatorYString (1) DOM(Extremely) High liquidity and credit quality (HQLA) indicator (Input Layer)
10CurrencyYString (3) DOMSubdomain for domain ISO-4217 Currency Codes (ISO4217 Domain) (Input Layer)
11Eligible_for_central_bank_funding_indicatorYString (1) DOMEligible for central bank fundung (Input Layer)
12Instrument_type_by_originYString (1) DOMInstrument type by origin
13Instrument_type_by_productYString (4) DOMType of instrument (Input layer)
14Litigation_statusYString (1) DOMLitigation status (Input Layer)
15Overdraft_indicatorYString (1) DOMOverdraft indicator (Input layer)
16Repayment_rightsYString (1) DOMRepayment rights (Input Layer)
17Revocable_indicatorYString (1) DOMRevocable indicator (Input Layer)
18Revolving_loan_indicatorYString (1) DOMRevolving loan indicator (Input Layer)
19Significant_asset_classYString (1) DOMSignificant asset class (Input Layer)
20Type_of_commitmentYString (2) DOMType of commitment (Input Layer)
21Type_of_riskYString (1) DOMType of risk (Input Layer)
22Commercial_real_estate_loan_indicatorYString (1) DOMCommercial real estate loan indicator
23Liability_encumbrance_residual_maturity_bandYStringDOMResidual maturity band (Input Layer)
24Syndication_or_sub-participation_member_instrument_indicatorYString (1) DOMSyndication or sub-participation member instrument indicator (Input layer)
25Accumulated_coverage_ratioDECIMALDOMReal numbers from 0 to 1 with 6 decimals
26Cash_reserve_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
27Cash_reserve_maturity_dateDateDOMAll possible dates
28Legal_final_maturity_dateDateDOMAll possible dates
29Minimum_reserve_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
30Nominal_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
31Notional_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
32Off-balance_sheet_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
33Original_cash_reserve_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
34Original_cash_reserve_amount_dateDateDOMAll possible dates
35Repurchase_transaction_Inception_dateDateDOMAll possible dates
36Settlement_dateDateDOMAll possible dates
37Unique_Product_IdentifierString (50) DOMString up to 50 characters
38Current_loan-to-value_ratioDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Instrument_identifierAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
6Credit_facility_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each credit facility.
7Balance_sheet_netting_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each a balance sheet netting.
8Financial_contract_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each financial contract.
9(Extremely)_high_liquidity_and_credit_quality_indicatorAn indicator specifying if an asset or collateral received is considered extremely high liquidity and credit quality (HQLA) eligible according to Regulation (EU) 680/2014, Annex 17 (Asset encumbrance), part 2, 2.1.2, column 035.
10CurrencyThe currency code according to ISO4217.
11Eligible_for_central_bank_funding_indicatorSpecifies if an object is eligible for central bank funding in the sense that it might be exchanged / provided as collateral for funding.
12Instrument_type_by_originA discriminator to distinguish instrument by their type of origin. For example instruments arising from financial contracts or instruments arising from credit facilities.
14Litigation_statusLitigation indicates the status of the litigation that the exposure is in.

Finrep Annex V,  Part 2.321, 322: An exposure shall be ‘in pre-litigation status’ where the debtor has been formally notified that the institution will take legal action against the debtor within a defined time period, unless certain contractual or other payment obligations are met. That shall also include cases where the contract has been terminated by the reporting institution because the debtor is in formal breach of the terms and conditions of the contract and the debtor has been notified accordingly, but no legal action against the debtor has formally been taken by the institution yet. Exposures classified as ‘in pre-litigation status’ can exit this classification if the outstanding amounts are paid or if they enter into litigation status as defined in the following paragraph. 
An exposure shall be ‘in litigation status’ where legal action against the debtor has formally been taken. This comprises cases where a court of law confirmed that formal judiciary proceedings have occurred or the judiciary system has been notified of the intention to commence legal proceedings.
15Overdraft_indicatorOverdraft indicator indicates whether the Transferable deposit is in an overdraft situation or not. 

Overdraft is a debit balance on a current account. As defined in point 2(1)(c) of the Table in Part 2 of Annex II to Regulation (EU) No 2021/379 (BSI)
16Repayment_rightsRepayment rights, e.g. redeemable at notice.
17Revocable_indicatorRevocable indicator is an indicator specifying if the Other commitment is revocable or not
18Revolving_loan_indicatorAn indicator specifying if an instrument is considered revolving or non-revolving.
19Significant_asset_classClassification into specific asset classes, like interest rate, equity, foreign exchange.
20Type_of_commitmentType of commitment.
21Type_of_riskType of financial risk Indicates the type of risk arising from exposures or transactions (e.g. credit risk or market risk) for Over the counter (OTC) Derivative instrument.
22Commercial_real_estate_loan_indicatorA Commercial real estate loan indicator distinguishes Loan derived data whether it is a commercial real estate loan or not.
24Syndication_or_sub-participation_member_instrument_indicatorSyndication or sub-participation member instrument indicator distinguishes Instrument between those that are a member of a syndicated loan or a sub-participation, and those that are not.
25Accumulated_coverage_ratioIt is calculated as the ratio between the accumulated impairments, respectively the accumulated negative changes in fair value due to credit risk related to a loan or advance as numerator, and the gross carrying amount of that loan or advance as denominator.
26Cash_reserve_amountThe cash reserve of a factoring operation.
27Cash_reserve_maturity_date The contractual maturity date of the cash reserve (acting as protection), which is the earliest date at which the cash reserve may terminate or be terminated, taking into account any agreements amending initial contracts.
28Legal_final_maturity_dateThe contractual maturity date of the asset or liability, taking into account any agreements amending initial contracts.
29Minimum_reserve_amountMinimum reserve iamount is the amount, expressed in the currency of the Loan on demand, that is put into reserve at another instition and should be seen as encumbered. This is very often at a Central bank, but could also be at the overall group of savings banks, which in its turn puts it up as minimum reserve at their cental bank.

This amount is calculated by the bank itself based on their respective interpretation and choices of the accounting standard.
30Nominal_amountHolding amount at nominal value
31Notional_amountThe notional amount on a financial instrument is the nominal or face amount that is used to calculate payments made on that instrument.
32Off-balance_sheet_amountTotal nominal amount of off-balance sheet exposures. This includes any commitment to lend before considering conversion factors and credit risk mitigation techniques. It is the amount that best represents the institution's maximum exposure to credit risk without taking into account any protection held or other credit enhancements.
33Original_cash_reserve_amountThe original cash reserve amount of a factoring operation.
34Original_cash_reserve_amount_dateOriginal cash reserve amount date.
35Repurchase_transaction_Inception_dateThe date on which the current contractual relationship originated, i.e. the date on which the contract agreement became binding for all parties.
36Settlement_dateThe date on which the conditions specified in the contract are or can be executed for the first time, i.e. the date on which financial instruments are initially exchanged or created.
37Unique_Product_IdentifierThe UPI’s purpose is to uniquely identify any OTC derivative product that an authority requires to be reported to a Trade Repository
38Current_loan-to-value_ratioCurrent loan-to-value ratio (LTV-C) means the sum of all loans or loan tranches secured by the borrower on a property at the reporting date relative to the current value of the property (ESRB), allocated per Loan derived data.
Indexes

Eligible_for_central_bank_funding_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable: To be used if central bank eligibility does not apply.
1Eligible for central bank funding: Specifies if an object is eligible for central bank funding in the sense that it might be exchanged / provided as collateral for funding.
2Not elibible for central bank funding: Specifies if an object is not eligible for central bank funding.
Instrument_type_by_origin
Values List
ValueDescription
3Instrument resulting directly from a Financial contract
4Instrument resulting from a Credit facility

Values List
ValueDescription
10OTC Option other than OTC Credit spread option
1003Reverse repurchase agreement instrument
1011Other commitment
1020Factoring
1022Other loan
1023Other trade receivables
120Transit items

1202On demand deposit not used for minimum reserve
13Financial guarantee instrument for a Debt security
130Suspence items
14Financial guarantee instrument not for a Debt security
140Other advance
162Open repurchase agreement instrument
163Term repurchase agreement instrument
2Equity instrument that is not a security
26Security against a fee borrowing and lending transaction
27Security against Security borrowing and lending transaction
380Forward
5Other OTC Derivative instrument
51Credit card debt
511Tranferable deposit
512Other overnight deposits
522Deposits with agreed maturity - other than counterpart liability to non-derecognised loans

8Other OTC Swap
80Finance leases

Litigation_status
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1In pre-litigation
2In litigation
3Not in litigation/pre-litigation
Overdraft_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Overdraft deposit
2Deposit not in overdraft
Repayment_rights
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1On demand or short notice: Instruments which are repayable on demand or at short notice at the request of the creditor.
2Other than on demand or short notice: Instruments subject to repayment rights other than on demand or at short notice.
Revocable_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Revocable
2Not revocable
Revolving_loan_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Revolving loan
2Not revolving loan
Significant_asset_class
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Interest rate
2Equity
3Credit
4Commodity
5Other
Type_of_commitment
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Forward deposits
10Loans commitments received
11Other commitments received
12To lend or to provide acceptance facilities at a below-market interest rate
13To lend or to provide acceptance facilities under pre-specified terms and conditions other than those at a below-market interest rate
14To lend or to provide acceptance facilities where the terms and conditions are not under pre-specified
15To provide guarantees
16To purchase securities
17For tender and performance guarantees
18Other Credit Facilities: Other
2Unpaid portion of partly-paid shares and securities
3Documentary credits issued or confirmed
4Trade finance off-balance sheet items
5Documentary credits in which underlying shipment acts as collateral and other self-liquidating transactions
6Warranties and indemnities’ (including tender and performance bonds) and ‘guarantees not having the character of credit substitutes
7Shipping guarantees, customs and tax bonds
8Note issuance facilities’ (NIFs) and ‘Revolving underwritings facilities’ (RUFs)
9Other off-balance sheet items
Type_of_risk
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not Applicable
1Interest rate risk: Type of risk in accordance with Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.129 (a)
2Equity risk: Type of risk in accordance with Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.129 (b)
3Foreign-exchange and gold risk: Type of risk in accordance with Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.129 (c)
4Credit risk: Type of risk in accordance with Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.129 (d)
5Commodities risk: Type of risk in accordance with Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.129 (e)
6Risk other than Interest rate risk, Equity risk, Foreign-exchange and gold risk, Credit risk, Commodities risk: Type of risk in accordance with Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.129 (f)
Commercial_real_estate_loan_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
1Commercial real estate loan
2Not a commercial real estate loan
Liability_encumbrance_residual_maturity_band
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not Applicable
12(3m,6m]
16(6m, 12m]
21(1y, 2y]
28(2y, 3y]
3(1d, 1w]
31(3y, 5y]

40Over 10 years
64[0d, 1d]
81> 7 days, <= 14 days
82> 2 weeks, <= 1 month
9(1m, 3m]
999Open Maturity

ValueDescription

Table NameInstrument_Collateral_assignment
Functional NameLoan and advance Collateral received assignment derived data
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionAn Instrument Collateral assignment is the combination of an Instrument and a Collateral that indicates which Instrument is protected by which Collateral.
Notes
Number Of Columns12
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Instrument_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYStringDOMSecurity position Balance sheet recognised type (Input Layer)
3Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
4Reference_datePFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Collateral_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Collateral_role_typePFYString (1) DOMCollateral role type (Input layer)
8Protection_allocated_valueYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
9Instrument_Collateral_assignment_typeString (1) DOMCollateral role type (Input layer)
10Allocated_collateral_value_uncapped_pre_haircutsDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
11Allocated_unused_collateral_valueDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
12Third_party_priority_claims_against_the_protectionDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Instrument_identifierThe instrument identifier of the involved loan.
2Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
4Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
5Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
6Collateral_identifierThe instrument identifier of the involved loan.
8Protection_allocated_valueThe maximum amount of the protection value that can be considered as credit protection for the instrument. The amount of the existing third parties or observed agent priority claims against the protection must be excluded in the protection allocated value. For protections that is eligible under Regulation (EU) No 575/2013, this value should be reported in accordance with Part 2 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
9Instrument_Collateral_assignment_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different types of Instrument Collateral received instrument assignments.
10Allocated_collateral_value_uncapped_pre_haircutsAllocated collateral value uncapped pre haircuts is an amount that is the value of the collateral that is not used for risk mitigations (uncapped) and before any haircuts have been applied, and that is for FINREP reporting "allocated" as unused amount to the Loan and advance.
11Allocated_unused_collateral_valueAllocated unused collateral value is an amount that expresses the protection value that is not used for risk mitigation and, for FINREP reporting purposes is allocated to a specific Loan and advance.
12Third_party_priority_claims_against_the_protectionThe maximum amount of any existing higher ranked liens with respect to third parties other than the observed agent against the protection.
Indexes
Index NameStateFunctionalSpatialExpressionColumn NameSort
Order

Accounting_standardASC
Reference_dateASC
Reporting_agent_identifierASC
Collateral_identifierASC
Collateral_role_typeASC

1Collateral received
2Collateral given

0Not applicable
1Collateral received

Table NameInstrument_Collateral_instrument_assignment
Functional NameInstrument Collateral instrument assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionAn Instrument Collateral  instrument assignment is the combination of an Instrument and a Collateral received instrument or Collateral given instrument that indicates which Instrument is protected by which Collateral received instrument or which instrument is being protected by which Collateral given instrument.
Notes
Number Of Columns8
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
3Accounting_standardPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
4Instrument_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
6Instrument_role_typePFYString (3) DOMInstrument role type (Input layer)
7Instrument_Collateral_received_instrument_assignment_typeYString (1) DOMInstrument Collateral instrument assignment type
8Protection_allocated_valueYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
2Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
4Instrument_identifierAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
5Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
7Instrument_Collateral_received_instrument_assignment_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different types of Instrument Collateral received instrument assignments.
8Protection_allocated_valueThe maximum amount of the protection value that can be considered as credit protection for the instrument. The amount of the existing third parties or observed agent priority claims against the protection must be excluded in the protection allocated value. For protections that is eligible under Regulation (EU) No 575/2013, this value should be reported in accordance with Part 2 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
Indexes

Order

Table NameInstrument_Entity_role_assignment
Functional NameInstrument Entity role assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionAn Instrument Entity role assignment is the combination of an Instrument and an Entity role that indicates which Instrument is assigned to which Entity role.
Notes
Number Of Columns13
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
2Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
3Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
4Party_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
5Instrument_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Party_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8Instrument_Entity_role_assignment_typeYString (2) DOMInstrument Entity role assignment type
9Litigation_statusYString (1) DOMLitigation status (Input Layer)
10Main_debtor_indicatorYString (1) DOMMain debtor indicator (Input Layer)
11Main_Deposit-taking_corporation_indicatorYString (1) DOMMain Deposit-taking corporation indicator (Input Layer)
12Main_lessee_indicatorYString (1) DOMMain lessee indicator (Input Layer)
13Joint_liability_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
2Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
4Party_role_typeThe organisation role.
5Instrument_identifierAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
6Party_identifierThe party identifier of the involved organisation.
7Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
8Instrument_Entity_role_assignment_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different assignments between instruments and party roles, for example Deposit-taking corporation deposit assignment.
9Litigation_statusFinrep Annex V,  Part 2.321, 322: An exposure shall be ‘in pre-litigation status’ where the debtor has been formally notified that the institution will take legal action against the debtor within a defined time period, unless certain contractual or other payment obligations are met. That shall also include cases where the contract has been terminated by the reporting institution because the debtor is in formal breach of the terms and conditions of the contract and the debtor has been notified accordingly, but no legal action against the debtor has formally been taken by the institution yet. Exposures classified as ‘in pre-litigation status’ can exit this classification if the outstanding amounts are paid or if they enter into litigation status as defined in the following paragraph. 
An exposure shall be ‘in litigation status’ where legal action against the debtor has formally been taken. This comprises cases where a court of law confirmed that formal judiciary proceedings have occurred or the judiciary system has been notified of the intention to commence legal proceedings.
10Main_debtor_indicatorAn indicator specifying if a debtor is the main debtor.
11Main_Deposit-taking_corporation_indicatorAn indicator specifying if a debtor is the main debtor.
12Main_lessee_indicatorAn indicator specifying if a debtor is the main debtor.
13Joint_liability_amountOutstanding nominal amount for which each debtor is liable in relation to a single instrument where there are two or more debtors.
Indexes
Index NameStateFunctionalSpatialExpressionColumn NameSort
Order

Reference_dateASC
Accounting_standardASC
Party_role_typeASC
Instrument_identifierASC
Party_identifierASC
Reporting_agent_identifierASC
Constraints
TypeColumn / Constraint NameDetails
Column LevelAccounting_standard

Table NameInstrument_Protection_arrangement_assignment
Functional NameInstrument Protection arrangement assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionAn Instrument Protection arrangement assignment is the combination of an Instrument and a Protection arrangement that indicates which Instrument is related to which Protection arrangement.
Notes
Number Of Columns8
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Accounting_standardPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
5Instrument_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Protection_arrangement_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Protection_arrangement_role_typePFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8Instrument_Protection_arrangement_assignment_typeYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Instrument_identifierAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
6Protection_arrangement_identifierAn identifier to univocally identify each protection arragement.
7Protection_arrangement_role_typeProtection arrangement role type distinguishes the Protection arrangement role according to their nature.
8Instrument_Protection_arrangement_assignment_typeInstrument Protection arrangement assignment type distinguishes Instrument Protection arrangement assignment according to their nature.
