Table NameInstrument_hedged_by_Exchange_tradable_derivative_position
Functional NameInstrument hedged by Exchange tradable derivative position
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionAn Instrument hedged by exchange tradable derivative position is the combination of an Instrument and an Exchange tradable derivative position as a hedge that indicates which Instrument is hedged by which Exchange tradable derivative position, acting in a the role of an Exchange tradable derivative position as a hedge.
Notes
Number Of Columns11
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
3Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
4Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
5Seller_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
6Seller_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Buyer_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
8Buyer_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
9Instrument_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore

11Exchange_tradable_derivative_position_role_typePFYString (2) DOMExchange tradable derivative position role type (Input Layer)
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
2Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
4Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
5Seller_role_typeThe seller role.
6Seller_identifierThe party identifier of the involved seller.
7Buyer_role_typeThe buyer party role.
8Buyer_identifierThe party identifier of the involved buyer.
9Instrument_identifierAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.

82General_allowances_for_bank_risk_affecting_carrying_amount_(nGAAP)DECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
83General_allowances_for_credit_risk_affecting_carrying_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
84Gross_carrying_amount_at_initial_recognitionDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
85Gross_carrying_amount_excluding_accrued_interestDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
86Haircuts_for_trading_position_valued_at_fair_valueDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
87Interest_rateDECIMAL (6)DOMReal numbers positive or negative with 6 decimals
88Interest_rate_capDECIMAL (6)DOMReal numbers positive or negative with 6 decimals
89Interest_rate_floorDECIMAL (6)DOMReal numbers positive or negative with 6 decimals
90Interest_rate_spread_/_marginDECIMAL (6)DOMReal numbers positive or negative with 6 decimals
91Market_risk_induced_accumulated_negative_value_adjustmentsDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
92Next_interest_rate_reset_dateDateDOMAll possible dates
93Original_protection_valueDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
94Protection_value_dateDateDOMAll possible dates
95Provisions_associated_with_off-balance_sheet_exposuresDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
96Settlement_dateDateDOMAll possible dates
97Start_date_of_the_periodDateDOMAll possible dates
98Accumulated_negative_changesDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
99Time_since_initial_recognitionString (2) DOMTime elapsed since initial recognition band
100Date_/_period_of_application_of_forbearance_measure_typeString (2) DOMDate / period of application of forbearance measure
101Derived_default_statusStringDOMDerived default status (Input Layer)
102Accumulated_partial_write-offsDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
103Accumulated_total_write-offsDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
104Gross_carrying_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
105Protection_valueDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Instrument_identifierAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
7Accounting_classificationAccounting portfolio where the asset or liability is recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP –under Regulation (EU) 2015/534 (ECB/2015/13) applied by the observed agent's legal entity.
8Amortisation_typeAmortisation type of the Financial asset instrument including principal and interest.
9Asset_encumbrance_source_indicatorAsset encumbrance source indicator signals whether the liabity or off-balance sheet item is a source of encumbrance for an asset or not.
10Available_for_encumbrance_indicatorAvailable for encumbrance indicator distinguishes the asset into being available for asset encumbrance and not being available for asset encumbrance.
11Balance_sheet_recognised_financial_asset_instrument_by_fair_value_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different types of Balance sheet recognised financial asset instruments by fair value.
12Balance_sheet_recognition_statusBalance sheet recognition of the financial asset.
13Collateral_instrument_reuse_indicatorCollateral instrument reuse indicator distinguishes between Collateral received instruments that are reused as Collateral given instruments and those that are not reused.
14Default_statusA discriminator to distinguish between default because unlikely to pay, 90/180 days past due or both according to Regulation (EU) 575/2013, Article 178.
15Eligible_as_guarantee_indicatorEligible as guarantee indicator distinguishes the Collateral received instrument data into those where the Collateral received instrument, which is a Credit Default Swap, is eligible to be the a guarantee, or is not eligible to be a guarantee.
16Encumbered_asset_indicatorEncumbered asset indicator signals whether the asset is encumbered or not.
17Exposure_with_recourse_indicatorExposure with recourse indicator specifies if a Financial asset instrument is an instrument with or without recourse.
18Fair_value_hierarchyLevel of fair value of a financial asset or liability according to the hierarchy defined in IFRS 13. Applicable to national GAAP based on BAD only if the allocation of instruments is measured at fair value according to different levels.
20Fiduciary_asset_indicatorIdentification of instruments in which the observed agent acts in its own name but on behalf of and with the risk borne by a third party.
21Financial_asset_instrument_type_by_CRR,_Article_123_(Retail_exposure)A discriminator to distinguish between financial assets that may potentially be classified as retail exposures and non-retail exposures.
22Financial_asset_instrument_type_by_fixed_interest_rateA discriminator to distinguish between financial asset instruments with and without fixed interest rate.
23Financial_asset_instrument_type_by_interest_rate_onlyA discriminator to distinguish between financial asset instruments with and without interest-only.
24Financial_asset_instrument_type_by_renegotiation_statusA discriminator to distinguish between financial asset instruments with and without renegotiations.
25Forbearance_measure_typeA discriminator to distinguish between refinanced debt, instruments with modified interest rate below market value and other modified terms.
26Held_for_sale_indicatorIndicates the Non-current assets and instruments in disposal groups classified as held for sale in accordance with IFRS5. Is not applicable under national GAAP based on BAD.
27Impairment_assessment_methodThe method by which the impairment is assessed, if the instrument is subject to impairment in accordance with applied accounting standards. Collective and individual methods are distinguished.
28Impairment_statusType of impairment recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP.
29Initial_impairment_statusInitial imapirment status is the impairment status, recorded in the beginning of the financial year or at the initial recognition of the impairment of the exposure after the beginning of the financial year.
30Interest_rate_reset_frequencyInterest rate reset frequency is the frequency at which the interest rate is reset after the initial fixed-rate period, if any for the Financial asset instrument.
31Low_credit_risk_indicator_for_FINREPLow credit risk indicator for FINREP signals loan or advance has low credit risk or not, according to the Banks' internal assessments.
32Multiple_forbearance_measures_in_place_indicatorForbearance measures were granted in addition to already existing forbearance measures. It concerns forborne exposures under probation to which forbearance measures were applied in addition to forbearance measures granted at an earlier point in time, without the exposure having cured in between.
33Non-performing_exit_criteria_met_indicatorNon-performing forborne exposures that failed to meet the non-performing exit criteria.
34Non-performing_prior_to_forbearance_indicatorAn indicator specifying if an exposure was non-performing prior to the extension of forbearance measures.
35Past_due_financial_asset_instrument_indicatorPast due financial instrument indicator distinguishes Financial instrument into whether or not its payments are being considered late, or past due.
36Payment_frequencyPayment frequency classifies the frequency of payments due, either of principal or interest, i.e. number of months between payments for the Financial asset instrument.
37Performing_forborne_exposure_under_probation_reclassified_from_non-performing_indicatorAn indicator specifying if an asset is classified as Non-performing forborne exposure under probation reclassified from non-performing or not according to Regulation (EU) 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.260.
38Performing_statusPerforming status indicates how the off-balance sheet item given instrument performes in accordance with Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
39Performing_status_reasonThe reason for the choice of Performing status
40Prudential_portfolio_typeA discriminator to distinguish between trading and banking (or non-trading) book.
41Purpose_typePurpose type is the classification of Financial asset instruments according to their purpose.
42Reference_rateReference rate used for the calculation of the actual interest rate.
43Specialised_lending_exposure_typeAn attribute  to specify what is the specialised lending type of an exposure.

According to CRR3 art 147 (8): Those exposures shall be assigned to the exposure class referred to in paragraph 2, point (c)(ii), and shall be categorised as follows: “project finance” (PF), “object finance” (OF), “commodity finance” (CF) and “income-producing real estate” (IPRE).’
44Subject_to_impairment_indicatorSubject to impairment indicator indicates whether the Financial asset instrument is subject to impairment or not.
45Subordinated_debt_indicatorSubordinated debt indicator specifies if the Financial asset instrument is considered as subordinated debt or not.
46Type_of_interest_rateType of interest rate classifies credit exposures based on the base rate for establishing the interest rate for each payment period.
47Type_of_protection_valuation_approachType of protection valuation approach.
48Transfer_between_impairment_stagesTransfer between impairment stages compares the impairment status at the beginning of the period (Initial impairment status) with the impairment status at the end of the period.
49Source_of_encumbranceType of transaction in which the exposure is encumbered in accordance with Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014. An asset will be treated as encumbered if it has been pledged or if it is subject to any form of arrangement to secure, collateralise or credit enhance any instrument from which it cannot be freely withdrawn.
50Time_past_due_bandA Time past due band distinguishes Financial asset instruments into time brackets according to how much time passed between the date on which the payment was due and the reference date. 
53Balance_sheet_recognised_financial_asset_instrumen_taken_into_possession_typeA discriminator to distinguish between Balance sheet recognised financial asset instrument obtained by taking possession and not obtained by taking into possesion
54Accrued_interestThe amount of accrued interest on loans at the reporting reference date as defined in Regulation (EU) No 1071/2013 (ECB/2013/33). In accordance with the general principle of accruals accounting, interest receivable on instruments should be subject to on-balance sheet recording as it accrues (i.e. on an accruals basis) rather than when it is actually received (i.e. on a cash basis).
55Accumulated_changes_in_fair_valueAmount of gains and losses from re-measurement of the asset accumulated from the initial recognition to the reference date.
56Accumulated_changes_in_fair_value_due_to_credit_riskAccumulated changes in fair value due to credit risk in accordance with Part 2.46 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
57Accumulated_fair_value_changes_of_the_exposure_due_to_credit_riskAccumulated changes in fair value due to credit risk in accordance with Part 2.46 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014 of the exposure that is derecognised because of the asset obtained by taking possession.

Note: This amount must only be reported when it is negative.
58Accumulated_impairmentThe amount of loss allowances that are held against or are allocated to the asset on the reporting reference date. This data attribute applies to assets subject to impairment under the applied accounting standard. Under IFRS, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected
credit losses;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected
credit losses.
Under GAAP, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to general allowances;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to specific allowances.
59Accumulated_recoveries_since_defaultThe total amount recovered since the date of default.
60Accumulated_write-offsAccumulated write-offs is the Cumulative amount of principal and past due interest of any non-balance sheet recognised financial asset instrument that the institution is no longer recognising because they are considered uncollectible, independently of the portfolio in which they were included. Write-offs could be caused both by reductions in the carrying amount of financial assets recognised directly in profit or loss and by reductions in the amounts of the allowance accounts for credit losses set off against the carrying amount of financial assets.
61Arrears_for_the_instrumentArrears for the instrument is the aggregate amount of principal, interest and any fee payment outstanding at the reporting date, which is contractually due and has not been paid (past due) for the Financial asset instrument. This amount is always to be reported. '0' is to be reported if the instrument was not past due on the reporting date.
62Balance_sheet_recognition_dateBalance sheet recognition date is a date on the asset obtained by taking possession  was taken into possession and thus recognised on the balance sheet.
63Carrying_amountThe carrying amount in accordance with Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
64Commitment_amount_at_inceptionCommitment amount at inception is the Observed agent's maximum exposure to credit risk on the inception date of the financial asset instrument, without taking into account any protection held or other credit enhancements. Total commitment amount on the inception date is established during the approval process and is intended to restrict an observed agent's amount of credit risk to a given counterparty for the relevant instrument.
65Credit_risk_induced_accumulated_negative_value_adjustmentsAccumulated negative value adjustments. Market risk induced
66Date_of_default_statusThe date on which the default status is considered to have arisen.
67Date_of_forbearance_statusThe date on which forbearance measure have been applied.
68Date_of_non-performing_statusThe date on which the non-performing status has been applied.
69Date_of_original_protection_valueThe date of the protection’s original value, i.e. the date on which the latest appraisal or valuation of the protection was carried out prior to its initial receipt as a credit protection.
70Date_of_renegotiation_statusThe date on which the renegotiation status is considered to have been established or changed.
71Date_past_dueDate past due is the date on which the Financial asset instrument became past due in accordance with Part 2.48 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014. This is the latest such date prior to the reporting reference date and it is to be reported if the instrument is past due on the reporting reference date.
72Derecognised_exposure_accumulated_impairmentAccumulated impairment is the amount of loss allowances that are held against or are allocated to the derecognised exposure on the reporting reference date for which the asset obtained by taking possession has been obtained.

This data attribute applies to assets subject to impairment under the applied accounting standard. Under IFRS, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected
credit losses;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected
credit losses.
Under GAAP, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to general allowances;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to specific allowances.
74End_date_of_interest-only_periodThe date on which the interest-only period ends. Interest-only is an instrument for which, for a contractually set period, only the interest on the principal balance is paid, with the principal balance remaining unchanged.
75Fair_valueFair value as defined in IFRS 13.9.
76Fair_value_changeFair value changes in the period and before taxes.
77Fair_value_changes_due_to_changes_in_credit_risk_before_purchaseFair value changes due to changes in credit risk before purchase is the difference between the outstanding nominal amount and the purchase price of the Financial asset instrument at the purchase date.
78Fair_value_changes_due_to_hedge_accountingChanges in the fair value of an asset or liability, which is a hedged item and measured at amortised cost, that are recognised in the carrying amount due to the application of hedge accounting (IFRS 9.6)
79Financial_asset_instrument_Inception_dateFinancial asset instrument Inception date is the date on which the current contractual relationship originated, i.e. the date on which the contract agreement of the Financial asset instrument became binding for all parties.
80Forbearance_measure_countNumber of times an exposure has been under forbearance measures. Exposures classified as forborne at the reporting reference date, to which forbearance measures have been applied at two, respectively more than two different points in time. That includes, among others, originally forborne exposures that exited the forborne status (cured forborne exposures), but were granted new forbearance measures after that.
81Freezing_period_in_daysFreezing period in days is the number of days (if any) for which the freezing period of the Past due financial asset instrument has been applied, in accordance with EBA guidelines 2016/07 paragraph 18.
82General_allowances_for_bank_risk_affecting_carrying_amount_(nGAAP)General allowances for bank risk affecting carrying amount (nGAAP)
83General_allowances_for_credit_risk_affecting_carrying_amountGeneral allowances for credit risk affecting carrying amount.
84Gross_carrying_amount_at_initial_recognitionGross carrying amount at the point in time of the initial recognition in the balance sheet 
85Gross_carrying_amount_excluding_accrued_interestGross carrying amount, as defined in IFRS 9 appendix A, excluding accrued interest
86Haircuts_for_trading_position_valued_at_fair_valueHaircut for trading position valued at fair value for the Balance sheet recognised financial asset instrument according to national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP). 
According to Annex V (5.29):
Where applicable under national GAAP based on BAD, ‘Haircuts for trading positions valued at fair value’ shall be reported. The haircuts decrease the value of trading assets and increase the value of trading liabilities.
87Interest_rateInterest rate is the annualised agreed rate or narrowly defined interest rate in accordance with Regulation (EU) No 1072/2013 of the European Central Bank (ECB/2013/34) that is applicable to the Financial asset instrument.
88Interest_rate_capMaximum value for the interest rate charged.
89Interest_rate_floorMinimum value for the interest rate charged.
90Interest_rate_spread_/_marginMargin or spread (expressed as a percentage) to add to the reference rate that is used for the calculation of the interest rate in basis points.
91Market_risk_induced_accumulated_negative_value_adjustmentsAccumulated negative value adjustments. Market risk induced
92Next_interest_rate_reset_dateNext interest rate reset date is the date that the next interest rate reset, as defined in Part 3 of Annex I to Regulation (EU) No 1071/2013 (ECB/2013/33), takes place for the Financial asset instrument. If the Financial asset instrument is not subject to a future interest rate reset, its legal final maturity date will be reported.
93Original_protection_valueThe protection’s fair value at the date when it was originally received as a credit protection.
94Protection_value_dateProtection value date is the date on which the latest appraisal or valuation of the protection was carried out prior to the reporting reference date.
95Provisions_associated_with_off-balance_sheet_exposuresThe amount of provisions for off-balance sheet amounts.
96Settlement_dateSettlement date is the date on which the conditions specified in the contract are or can be executed for the first time, i.e. the date on which the financial asset instrument is initially exchanged or created.
97Start_date_of_the_periodThe start date of the period.
98Accumulated_negative_changesAccumulated negative changes shall be the difference, at the level of the individual asset obtained by taking in to possession  item, between the value at initial recognition of the asset  and the carrying amount at the reporting reference date, where that difference is negative. (FINREP Annex V)
99Time_since_initial_recognitionTime elapsed since initial recognition band classifies time elapsed since initial recognition in the balance sheet into bands used for FINREP reporting.
100Date_/_period_of_application_of_forbearance_measure_typeDate / period of application of forbearance measure type classifies if the forbearance measure date is applicable to the current reporting period or not.
101Derived_default_statusDerived default status is the default status where all specific statusses of default for the Financial asset instrument have been mapped to either "Default" or "Not in default".
102Accumulated_partial_write-offsAccording to FinRep Annex 5: "the accumulated partial (...) amount as at the reference date of principal and accrued past due interest and fees of any debt instrument that has been de-recognised to date (...) because the institution has no reasonable expectations of recovering the contractual cash flows."
103Accumulated_total_write-offsAccording to FinRep Annex 5: "the accumulated (...) total amount as at the reference date of principal and accrued past due interest and fees of any debt instrument that has been de-recognised to date (...) because the institution has no reasonable expectations of recovering the contractual cash flows."

104Gross_carrying_amountGross carrying amount as defined in Annex V 5.2.

Instrument_role_type
Values List
ValueDescription
101Non-balance sheet recognised financial asset instrument according to International Financial Reporting Standard (IFRS)
102Non-balance sheet recognised financial asset instrument according to national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP)
12Non-balance sheet recognised financial liability instrument
34Balance sheet recognised financial asset instrument according to national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP)
35Balance sheet recognised financial asset instrument according to International Financial Reporting Standard (IFRS)
37Non-fair-valued balance sheet recognised financial liability instrument
38Over the counter (OTC) Credit default swap received as collateral instrument
39Other collateral received instrument
46Fair-valued Balance sheet recognised financial liability instrument according to International Financial Reporting Standard (IFRS)
47Fair-valued balance sheet recognised financial liability instrument according to national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP)
501Forborne off-balance sheet item given instrument
502Non-Forborne off-balance sheet item given instrument
6Off-balance sheet item received instrument
8Collateral given instrument
Accounting_classification
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
10 nGAAP: Loans and receivables
12nGAAP: Held-to-maturity investments
14IFRS: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits in accordance with IFRS.
2IFRS: Financial assets held for trading: Financial assets held for trading in accordance with IFRS.
21IFRS: Financial liabilities measured at amortised cost: Financial liabilities measured at amortised cost in accordance with IFRS
23IFRS: Financial liabilities held for trading: Financial liabilities held for trading in accordance with IFRS
25IFRS: Financial liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with IFRS
3nGAAP: Trading Financial assets: Trading financial assets in accordance with national GAAP.
31nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial liabilities measured at a cost-based method: Non-trading non-derivative financial liabilities measured at a cost-based method accordance with national GAAP based on BAD
33nGAAP: Trading financial liabilities: Trading financial liabilities in accordance with national GAAP based on BAD
35nGAAP: Financial liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with nationa GAAP based on BAD
4IFRS: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial assets measured at fair value through profit and loss and designated as such upon initial recognition or subsequently in accordance with IFRS, except those classified as financial assets held for trading.
41IFRS: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with IFRS.
45nGAAP: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits in accordance with national GAAP.
46nGAAP: Financial assets held for trading
47nGAAP: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with national GAAP.
48nGAAP: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss
5nGAAP: Available-for-sale financial assets
6IFRS: Financial assets at amortised cost: Financial assets measured at amortised cost in accordance with IFRS.
64nGAAP: financial assets at fair value or strict LOCOM
7nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value through profit or loss: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity in accordance with national GAAP.
711Accounting portfolios for financial assets other than classified as held for sale, excluding financial assets held for trading, trading financial assets and cash and cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits
73nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets. LOCOM: nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets at LOCOM
74nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets. Other than LOCOM
76nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method. LOCOM: nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method at LOCOM
77nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method. Other than LOCOM
8IFRS: Financial assets at fair value through other comprehensive income: Financial assets measured at fair value through other comprehensive income due to business model and cash-flows characteristics in accordance with IFRS.
83Investments in subsidiaries, joint ventures and associates.
851nGAAP: Accounting portfolios for trading financial assetss. Cost based method or LOCOM
852nGAAP: Accounting portfolios for trading financial liabilities. Cost based method or LOCOM
9nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity in accordance with national GAAP.
90Under IFRS 9 impairment: Off balance sheet accounting classification: under IFRS 9 impairment
911Measured under IAS 37: Off balance sheet accounting classification: measured under IAS 37
912Measured under IFRS 4: Off balance sheet accounting classification: measured under IFRS 4
92Measured at fair value through profit or loss: Off balance sheet accounting classification: IFRS 9 fair valued commitments and financial guarantees
93Under nGAAP: Off balance sheet accounting classification: measured under nGAAP based on BAD
Amortisation_type
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1French: Amortisation in which the total amount - principal plus interest - repaid in each instalment is the same.
2German: Amortisation in which the first instalment is interest-only and the remaining instalments are constant, including capital amortisation and interest.
3Fixed amortisation schedule: Amortisation in which the principal amount repaid in each instalment is the same.
4Bullet: Amortisation in which the full principal amount is repaid in the last instalment.
5Amortisation types other than French, German, Fixed amortisation schedule or bullet: Other amortisation schedule applied than the ones specified.
Asset_encumbrance_source_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Asset encumbrance source
2Not an asset encumbrance source
Available_for_encumbrance_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Available for encumbrance
2Not-available for encumbrance
Balance_sheet_recognised_financial_asset_instrument_by_fair_value_type
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
42Fair-valued balance sheet recognised financial asset instrument
43Non-fair-valued balance sheet recognised financial asset instrument
Balance_sheet_recognition_status
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Entirely recognised: Instrument entirely recognised, in accordance with Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
2Recognised to the extent of the institution's continuing involvement
4Partially derecognised: Instrument partially derecognised
Collateral_instrument_reuse_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Collateral instrument used once
2Collateral instrument reused
Default_status
Values List
ValueDescription
18Default because both unlikely to pay and more than 90/180 days past due
19Default because unlikely to pay
20Default because more than 90/180 days past due
Eligible_as_guarantee_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Not eligible as guarantee
2Eligible as guarantee
Encumbered_asset_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Not encumbered asset
100Encumbered asset
Exposure_with_recourse_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Recourse
2No recourse
Fair_value_hierarchy
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Level 1: Level 1 inputs used for the measurement of fair value. Level 1 inputs are quoted prices in active markets for identical assets or liabilities that the entity can access at the measurement date
2Level 2: Level 2 inputs used for the measurment of fair value. Level 2 inputs are inputs other than quoted prices included within Level 1 that are observable for the asset or liability, either directly or indirectly
3Level 3: Level 3 inputs used for the measurment of fair value. Level 3 inputs are unobservable inputs for the asset or liability
Fair_value_option_designation
Values List
ValueDescription
1Accounting mismatch
2Management on a fair value basis
5Management of credit risk. Upon designation
6Management of credit risk. After the designation
Fiduciary_asset_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Fiduciary asset
2Non-fiduciary asset
Financial_asset_instrument_type_by_CRR,_Article_123_(Retail_exposure)
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
24Performing non-retail exposure class financial asset instrument
25Non-performing non-retail exposure class financial asset instrument
28Default financial asset instrument individually assessed
30Performing financial asset instrument debtor assessed
31Non-performing financial asset instrument debtor assessed
32Performing non-default financial asset instrument individually assessed
33Non-performing non-default financial asset instrument individually assessed
Financial_asset_instrument_type_by_fixed_interest_rate
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
18Fixed interest financial asset instrument
19Non-fixed interest financial asset instrument
Financial_asset_instrument_type_by_interest_rate_only
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
20Interest-only financial asset instrument
21Non-interest-only financial asset instrument
Financial_asset_instrument_type_by_renegotiation_status
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
14Renegotiated financial asset instrument without forbearance measure
15Renegotiated financial asset instrument with forbearance measure
17Non-renegotiated financial asset instrument
Forbearance_measure_type
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Grace period/payment moratorium
10Forborne: instruments with modified interest rate below market conditions
11Forborne: instruments with modified interest rate not below market conditions
3Extension of maturity/term
4Rescheduled payments
5Debt forgiveness
6Debt asset swaps
7Other forbearance measures
8Forborne: Refinanced debt
9Forborne: instruments with other modified terms and conditions
Held_for_sale_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Held for sale: The member indicates that the instrument is held for sale
2Not held for sale: The member indicates that the instrument is not held for sale
Impairment_assessment_method
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Collectively assessed
2Individually assessed
Impairment_status
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
211General allowances for credit risk (GAAP)
212General allowances for banking risk (GAAP)
23Stage 1 (IFRS): To be used if the instrument is not impaired and a loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected credit losses is raised against the instrument under IFRS. Only for instruments subject to impairment under IFRS 9.
24Stage 2 (IFRS): To be used if the instrument is not impaired and a loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected credit losses is raised against the instrument under IFRS. Only for instruments subject to impairment under IFRS 9.
25Stage 3 (IFRS): To be used if the instrument is impaired and a loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected credit losses is raised against the instrument under IFRS. Only for instruments subject to impairment under IFRS 9.
26Specific allowances (GAAP): To be used if the instrument is subject to impairment in accordance with an applied accounting standard other than IFRS 9 and specific loss allowances are raised, irrespective of whether these allowances are individually or collectively assessed (impaired
27Purchased or originated credit-impaired instruments (POCI) (IFRS)
Interest_rate_reset_frequency
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Annual
12Other than overnight, monthly, quarterly, half yearly, annually or at creditor discretion
16Overnight
18Quarterly
19Semi-annually
3At creditor discretion
8Monthly

Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Low credit risk instrument
2Non-low credit risk instrument

Values List

Values List

Payment_frequency
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Annual
15Other than monthly, quarterly, half yearly, annually, bullet or zero coupon
18Quarterly
19Semi-annually
22Zero-coupon: Amortisation in which the full principal amount and interest is repaid in the last instalment.
4Bullet: Amortisation in which the full principal amount is repaid in the last instalment regardless of the interest payment frequency.
8Monthly
Performing_forborne_exposure_under_probation_reclassified_from_non-performing_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Performing forborne exposure under probation
2Performing forborne exposure not under probation
Performing_status
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Non-performing
11Performing
Performing_status_reason
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Failed reclassification to performing at end of probation period
2Exited from Non-performing exposure (NPE) in the last 12 months
Prudential_portfolio_type
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Trading book
2Non-trading book
Purpose_type
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Credit for consumption: Credit for consumption (a loan granted predominantly for the purpose of personal consumption of goods and services), in accordance with Part 2 of Annex II of Regulation (EU) No 1071/2013 (ECB/2013/33).
12Residential real estate purchase: Financing of residential property. Residential property is defined in Article 4(1)(75) of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.
13Commercial real estate purchase: Financing of real estate property other than residential property.
19Other purposes: Purposes other than consumption, debt financing, residential and commercial real estate purchase, margin lending, imports, exports, construction investment and working capital facility.
4Margin lending: Instruments in which an institution extends credit in connection with the purchase, sale, carrying or trading of securities. Margin lending instruments do not include other loans that are secured by collateral in the form of securities.
5Debt financing: Financing of outstanding or maturing debt. This includes debt refinancing.
6Imports: Financing of goods and services (purchases, barter and/or gifts) from non-residents to residents.
7Exports: Financing of goods and services (sales, barter and/or gifts) from residents to non-residents.
8Construction investment: Financing of construction of buildings, infrastructure and industrial facilities.
9Working capital facility: Financing the cash flow management of an organisation.

Subject_to_impairment_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Subject to impairment
2Not subject to impairment
Subordinated_debt_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Subordinated debt
2Not subordinated debt
Type_of_interest_rate
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Fixed: Scheme defining the interest rates during the life of the exposure which only includes constant rates - numeric constant rate known with certainty at the inception of the exposure - and where the interest rates apply to the whole exposure. The scheme may contain more than one constant interest rate to be applied at different periods during the life of the exposure (e.g. loan with a constant interest rate during the initial fixed-rate period, which then changes to a different interest rate, which is still constant, and which was known at the inception of the exposure).
2Variable: Scheme defining the interest rates during the life of the exposure which only includes interest rates based on the evolution of another variable (the reference variable) and where the interest rate applies to the whole exposure.
3Mixed: Interest rate class other than fixed or variable.
Type_of_protection_valuation_approach
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Counterparty estimation: Valuation method whereby the valuation is carried out by the provider of the protection.
2Creditor valuation: Valuation method whereby the valuation is carried out by the creditor: valuation undertaken by an external or staff appraiser who possesses the necessary qualifications, ability and experience to execute a valuation and who is not independent from the credit decision process.
3Mark-to-market: Valuation method whereby the protection value is based on unadjusted quoted prices for identical assets and liabilities in an active market.
4Other type of valuation: Other type of valuation not included in any other valuation categories.
5Third-party valuation: Valuation method in which the valuation is provided by an appraiser who is independent from the credit decision process.
Source_of_encumbrance
Values List
ValueDescription
10Debt securities issued - covered bonds securities: Covered bonds securities issued in accordance with the EBA’s implementing technical standards on asset encumbrance reporting as referred to in Article 99(5) and Article 100 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.
11Debt securities issued - asset-backed securities: Asset-backed securities (ABS) issued in accordance with the EBA’s implementing technical standards on asset encumbrance reporting as referred to in Article 99(5) and Article 100 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.
12Debt securities issued - other than covered bonds and ABSs: Debt securities issued other than covered bonds and ABSs in accordance with the EBA’s implementing technical standards on asset encumbrance reporting as referred to in Article 99(5) and Article 100 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.
13Other sources of encumbrance: Other sources of encumbrance in accordance with the EBA’s implementing technical standards on asset encumbrance reporting as referred to in Article 99(5) and Article 100 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.
14Repurchase agreements
15Short position
16Loan commitments received
17Financial guarantees received
18Securities borrowed with non-cash collateral
6Exchange traded derivatives: Exchange traded derivatives in accordance with the EBA’s implementing technical standards on asset encumbrance reporting as referred to in Article 99(5) and Article 100 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.
7Over-the-counter derivatives: Over-the-counter derivatives in accordance with the EBA’s implementing technical standards on asset encumbrance reporting as referred to in Article 99(5) and Article 100 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.
9Deposits other than repurchase agreements: Deposits other than repurchase agreements in accordance with the EBA’s implementing technical standards on asset encumbrance reporting as referred to in Article 99(5) and Article 100 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.
Protection_value_type
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not Applicable
1Notional amount
2Fair value
3Market value
4Long-term sustainable value
5Other protection value

ValueDescription
0Not Applicable
12(3m,6m]
16(6m, 12m]
21(1y, 2y]
28(2y, 3y]

36(5y, 10y]
40Over 10 years

81> 7 days, <= 14 days
82> 2 weeks, <= 1 month
9(1m, 3m]
999Open Maturity

Table NameInterest_rate_risk_hedge_portfolio
Functional NameInterest rate risk hedge portfolio
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionInterest rate risk hedge portfolio are the portfolio of instruments whose hedged postions need to be reported as one with the rows called 'Fair value changes of the hedged items in portfolio hedge of interest rate risk'.

See https://www.ifrs.org/content/dam/ifrs/meetings/2010/september/iasb/vbfi0910b14bobs.pdf
Notes
Number Of Columns7
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level
4Accounting_standardPYString (1) DOMAccounting framework
5Domestic_activities_indicatorPYString (1) DOMDomestic activities indicator
6Hedged_portfolio_asset_or_liability_indicatorPYString (1) DOMHedged portfolio asset or liability indicator
7Hedged_items_fair_value_changesYDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Domestic_activities_indicatorDomestic activities indicator distinguishes the Interest rate risk hedge portfolio between domestic and non-domestic activities from the perspective of the reporting agent.
6Hedged_portfolio_asset_or_liability_indicatorHedged portfolio  asset or liability indicator classifies  the Interest rate risk hedge portfolio  into assets or liabilities.
7Hedged_items_fair_value_changesFair value changes of the hedged items in portfolio hedge of interest rate risk
Indexes
Index NameStateFunctionalSpatialExpressionColumn NameSort
Order

Hedged_portfolio_asset_or_liability_indicatorASC

2Group consolidation level

ValueDescription
1National GAAP not consistent with IFRS

2Hedged portfolio liabilities

DescriptionA Internal group role is a role an Internal group may act in.

Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear

Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Group_role_typePYString (1) DOMGroup role type
2Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
3Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Group_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
6Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
7Accounting_standard_for_consolidated_reportingYString (1) DOMAccounting framework
8Accounting_standard_for_solo_reportingYString (1) DOMAccounting framework

11Finrep_reporting_schemeYStringDOMFinrep reporting scheme
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Group_role_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a group may act in.
2Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
3Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
4Group_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each group.
5Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
7Accounting_standard_for_consolidated_reportingAccounting standard for consolidated reporting is the accounting standard used by the Reporting agent internal group role for cons reporting.
8Accounting_standard_for_solo_reportingAccounting standard for solo reporting is the accounting standard used by the Reporting agent internal group role for solo reporting.
9Accounting_standard_for_statistical_reportingAccounting standard for statistical reporting is the accounting standard used by the Reporting agent internal group role to report statistical data.

Table NameInvestment_property_taken_into_possession
Functional NameInvestment property taken into possession 
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionInvestment property taken into possession is an investment property that was pledged by the debtor as collateral, but was obtained in exchange for the cancellation of debt, whether on a voluntary basis or as part of legal proceedings.
Notes
Number Of Columns20
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
4Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
5Held_for_sale_indicatorPFYString (1) DOMHeld for sale indicator (Input Layer)
6Measurement_methodPYString (1) DOMMeasurement method
7Subject_to_operating_lease_indicatorPYString (1) DOMSubject to operating lease indicator
8Investment_property_taken_into_possession_typePYString (2) DOMInvestment property type
9Balance_sheet_recognition_datePYDateDOMAll possible dates
10Non-financial_asset_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
11Accumulated_fair_value_changes_of_the_exposure_due_to_credit_riskYDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
12Derecognised_exposure_accumulated_impairmentYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
13Derecognised_exposure_gross_carrying_amountYDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
14Gross_carrying_amountYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
15Gross_carrying_amount_at_initial_recognitionYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
16Number_of_Investment_properties_taken_into_possessionYREALDOMNon-negative real numbers with 0 decimals
17Planning_permission_for_development_indicatorYString (1) DOMPlanning permission indicator (Input Layer)
18Under_construction_or_development_indicatorYString (1) DOMUnder construction or development indicator (Input Layer)
19Accumulated_negative_changesYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
20Time_since_initial_recognitionYString (2) DOMTime elapsed since initial recognition band
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Held_for_sale_indicatorHeld for sale  indicates the Non-current assets and instruments in disposal groups classified as held for sale in accordance with IFRS5. Is not applicable under national GAAP based on BAD.
6Measurement_methodMeasurement method identifies the type of measurement method according to IAS (See IAS 16.30, 16.31 and IAS 40.33-55, 76). It distinguishes into their cost or revaluation model. 
7Subject_to_operating_lease_indicatorAn indicator to specify if an object is subject to operating lease or not.
9Balance_sheet_recognition_dateBalance sheet recognition date is a date on the asset obtained by taking possession  was taken into possession and thus recognised on the balance sheet.
11Accumulated_fair_value_changes_of_the_exposure_due_to_credit_riskAccumulated changes in fair value due to credit risk in accordance with Part 2.46 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014 of the exposure that is derecognised because of the asset obtained by taking possession.

Note: This amount must only be reported when it is negative.
12Derecognised_exposure_accumulated_impairmentAccumulated impairment is the amount of loss allowances that are held against or are allocated to the derecognised exposure on the reporting reference date for which the asset obtained by taking possession has been obtained.

This data attribute applies to assets subject to impairment under the applied accounting standard. Under IFRS, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected
credit losses;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected
credit losses.
Under GAAP, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to general allowances;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to specific allowances.
14Gross_carrying_amountGross carrying amount at the point in time of the initial recognition in the balance sheet 
15Gross_carrying_amount_at_initial_recognitionGross carrying amount at the point in time of the initial recognition in the balance sheet 
17Planning_permission_for_development_indicatorPlanning permission for development indicator distinguishes Land excluding agriculture based on whether planning permission for development applies or not.
18Under_construction_or_development_indicatorUnder construction or development indicator indicates whether the property is under construction or development.

Finrep Annex V. Part 2.350, 352(a). With regard to collateral in the form of immovable property (a) immovable property that is under construction or development.
19Accumulated_negative_changesAccumulated negative changes shall be the difference, at the level of the individual asset obtained by taking in to possession  item, between the value at initial recognition of the asset  and the carrying amount at the reporting reference date, where that difference is negative. (FINREP Annex V)
20Time_since_initial_recognitionTime elapsed since initial recognition band classifies time elapsed since initial recognition in the balance sheet into bands used for FINREP reporting.
Indexes
Index NameStateFunctionalSpatialExpressionColumn NameSort
Order

Accounting_consolidation_levelASC

Measurement_methodASC
Subject_to_operating_lease_indicatorASC

Balance_sheet_recognition_dateASC

Constraints
TypeColumn / Constraint NameDetails
Column LevelAccounting_consolidation_level
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Solo consolidation level
2Group consolidation level
Accounting_standard
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1National GAAP not consistent with IFRS
2IFRS
3National GAAP consistent with IFRS
Held_for_sale_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Held for sale: The member indicates that the instrument is held for sale
2Not held for sale: The member indicates that the instrument is not held for sale
Measurement_method
Values List
ValueDescription
1Cost model: IAS 17.49; IAS 16.30, 73(a)(d)
2Fair value model
3Revaluation model: IAS 17.49; IAS 16.31, 73(a)(d)
Subject_to_operating_lease_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
1Subject to operating lease: The asset is subject to operating lease
2Not Subject to operating lease: The asset is not subject to operating lease
Investment_property_taken_into_possession_type
Values List
ValueDescription
1Movable property
2Residential Immovable property
31Commercial immovable property related to land (excluding agricultural land)
32Commercial immovable property other than related to land (excluding agricultural land)
4Other Immovable property
Planning_permission_for_development_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Land with planning permission for developnment
2Land without planning permission for development
Under_construction_or_development_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Under construction or development
2Not under construction or development
Time_since_initial_recognition
Values List
ValueDescription
27<= 2 years
29> 2 years <= 5 years
37> 5 years

Table NameLinked_enterprise_assignment
Functional NameLinked enterprise assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Linked enterprise is a combination of a Central bank and private sector company and a Central bank and private sector company that indicates which Central bank and private sector company is linked to which Central bank and private sector company in the sense of Commission Recommendation 2003/361/EC.
Notes
Number Of Columns7
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3Central_bank_and_private_sector_company_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
5Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
6Linked_enterprise_Party_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
7Linked_enterprise_Party_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Central_bank_and_private_sector_company_identifierThe party identifier of the involved linked enterprise.
4Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
6Linked_enterprise_Party_role_typeLinked enterprise Party role type is the role type identifying the central bank and private sector company role type as Linked enterprise in the Linked enterprise assignment.
7Linked_enterprise_Party_identifierThe party identifier of the involved linked enterprise.

Table NameLoan_and_advance_as_a_hedge
Functional NameLoan and advance as a hedge
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionLoan and advance as a hedge is a Loan and advance role where the Loan  and advance instrument acts as a non-derivative hedge.
Notes
Number Of Columns7
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Accounting_standardPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
5Instrument_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Accounting_hedge_indicatorYString (1) DOMAccounting hedge indicator
7Type_of_hedgeYString (2) DOMType of hedge
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Instrument_identifierAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
6Accounting_hedge_indicatorAn indicator specifying if the hedge is considered an accounting hedge or not.
7Type_of_hedgeType of hedge applicable under the relevant accounting standard.

Type_of_hedge
Values List
ValueDescription
1Fair value hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (a)
11Hedges other than cash flow hedge and hedge of net investment in a foreign operation: Economic hedge
12Economic hedge. With use of fair value option
13Economic hedge. Other
2Cash flow hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (b)
3Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (c)
4Portfolio fair value hedges of interest rate risk: Hedging relationship as defined in IAS39.AG 114-132
5Portfolio cash flow hedges of interest rate risk: Hedging relationship as defined in IAS39.AG 114-132
6Cost-price hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.114. This hedging relationship is applicable only under nGAAP based on BAD
7Other than Fair value hedge, Cash flow hedge, Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation, Portfolio fair value hedges of interest rate risk, Portfolio cash flow hedges of interest rate risk, Cost-price hedge: Other applicable hedging relationship for nGAAP based on BAD reporters

Table NameLong/Short_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position
Functional NameLong/Short balance sheet recognised security position
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Long/Short balance sheet recognised security position is a balance sheet recognised security position that is either long or short.

Notes
Number Of Columns18
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Investor_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2Investor_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
3Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
4Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
6Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
7Security_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8Long/Short_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_typePYStringDOMLong/Short balance sheet recognised security position type (Input Layer)
9Security_position_Balance_sheet_recognised_typePFYStringDOMSecurity position Balance sheet recognised type (Input Layer)
10Fair_value_hierarchyYString (1) DOMFair value hierarchy (Input Layer)
11Held_for_sale_indicatorYString (1) DOMHeld for sale indicator (Input Layer)
12Security_position_role_typeYString (1) DOMSecurity position role type
13Short_position_accounting_classificationYString (2) DOMAccounting classification for liabilities
14Accumulated_changes_in_fair_valueDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
15Carrying_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
16Fair_valueDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
17Fair_value_changeDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
18Haircuts_for_trading_position_valued_at_fair_valueDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Investor_identifierAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
2Investor_role_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
3Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
4Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
5Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
7Security_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
10Fair_value_hierarchyLevel of fair value of a financial asset or liability according to the hierarchy defined in IFRS 13. Applicable to national GAAP based on BAD only if the allocation of instruments is measured at fair value according to different levels.
11Held_for_sale_indicatorHeld for sale  indicates the Non-current assets and instruments in disposal groups classified as held for sale in accordance with IFRS5. Is not applicable under national GAAP based on BAD.
12Security_position_role_typeSecurity position role type distinguishes the Security position according to the role it can have, asset, liability, undetermined. This can be derived from the type of security position.

Nota Bene: there can be only one role for the Security position at any given moment in time.
13Short_position_accounting_classificationAccounting portfolio where the asset or liability is recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP –under Regulation (EU) 2015/534 (ECB/2015/13) applied by the observed agent's legal entity.
14Accumulated_changes_in_fair_valueAmount of gains and losses from re-measurement of the asset accumulated from the initial recognition to the reference date.
15Carrying_amountThe carrying amount in accordance with Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
16Fair_valueFair value as defined in IFRS 13.9.
17Fair_value_changeFair value changes in the period and before taxes.
18Haircuts_for_trading_position_valued_at_fair_valueHaircut for trading position valued at fair value. 
According to Annex V (5.29):
Where applicable under national GAAP based on BAD, ‘Haircuts for trading positions valued at fair value’ shall be reported. The haircuts decrease the value of trading assets and increase the value of trading liabilities.

Accounting_consolidation_level
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Solo consolidation level
2Group consolidation level
Accounting_standard
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1National GAAP not consistent with IFRS
2IFRS
3National GAAP consistent with IFRS
Long/Short_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_type
Values List
ValueDescription
3Long balance sheet recognised security position International Financial Reporting Standard (IFRS)
4Long balance sheet recognised security position national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP)
5Short balance sheet recognised security position International Financial Reporting Standard (IFRS)
6Short balance sheet recognised security position national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP)
Security_position_Balance_sheet_recognised_type
Values List
ValueDescription
1Balance sheet recognised security position
3Non-balance sheet recognised debt security position
4Non-balance sheet recognised equity or fund security position
Fair_value_hierarchy
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Level 1: Level 1 inputs used for the measurement of fair value. Level 1 inputs are quoted prices in active markets for identical assets or liabilities that the entity can access at the measurement date
2Level 2: Level 2 inputs used for the measurment of fair value. Level 2 inputs are inputs other than quoted prices included within Level 1 that are observable for the asset or liability, either directly or indirectly
3Level 3: Level 3 inputs used for the measurment of fair value. Level 3 inputs are unobservable inputs for the asset or liability
Held_for_sale_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Held for sale: The member indicates that the instrument is held for sale
2Not held for sale: The member indicates that the instrument is not held for sale
Security_position_role_type
Values List
ValueDescription
1Asset security position
2Liability security position
3Undetermined asset or liability security position
Short_position_accounting_classification
Values List
ValueDescription
23IFRS: Financial liabilities held for trading: Financial liabilities held for trading in accordance with IFRS
33nGAAP: Trading financial liabilities: Trading financial liabilities in accordance with national GAAP based on BAD

kind		Domain NameFormula

NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
2Investor_identifierAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
3Investor_role_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
4Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
5Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
6Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
8Security_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
10Accounting_hedge_indicatorAn indicator specifying if the hedge is considered an accounting hedge or not.
11Type_of_hedgeType of hedge applicable under the relevant accounting standard.

ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1National GAAP not consistent with IFRS

Long/Short_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_type
Values List
ValueDescription
3Long balance sheet recognised security position International Financial Reporting Standard (IFRS)
4Long balance sheet recognised security position national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP)
5Short balance sheet recognised security position International Financial Reporting Standard (IFRS)
6Short balance sheet recognised security position national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP)
Accounting_hedge_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
1Accounting hedge
Type_of_hedge
Values List
ValueDescription
1Fair value hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (a)
11Hedges other than cash flow hedge and hedge of net investment in a foreign operation: Economic hedge
12Economic hedge. With use of fair value option
13Economic hedge. Other
2Cash flow hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (b)
3Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (c)
4Portfolio fair value hedges of interest rate risk: Hedging relationship as defined in IAS39.AG 114-132
5Portfolio cash flow hedges of interest rate risk: Hedging relationship as defined in IAS39.AG 114-132
6Cost-price hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.114. This hedging relationship is applicable only under nGAAP based on BAD
7Other than Fair value hedge, Cash flow hedge, Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation, Portfolio fair value hedges of interest rate risk, Portfolio cash flow hedges of interest rate risk, Cost-price hedge: Other applicable hedging relationship for nGAAP based on BAD reporters

Table NameLong_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_collateral_received_assignment
Functional NameLong balance sheet recognised security position Collateral received assignment derived data
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionLong balance sheet recognised security position collateral received assigment is the combination of Long balance sheet recognised security position and  collateral received stating which Long balance sheet recognised security position is protected by which Collateral.
Notes
Number Of Columns14
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYStringDOMSecurity position Balance sheet recognised type (Input Layer)
4Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
5Collateral_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Collateral_role_typePFYString (1) DOMCollateral role type (Input layer)
7Security_position_Balance_sheet_recognised_typePFYStringDOMSecurity position Balance sheet recognised type (Input Layer)
8Investor_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
9Investor_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
10Long_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_Security_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
11Long/Short_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_typePFYStringDOMLong/Short balance sheet recognised security position type (Input Layer)
12Allocated_unused_collateral_valueYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
13Protection_allocated_valueYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
14Allocated_collateral_value_uncapped_pre_haircutsDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Collateral_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each collateral.
6Collateral_role_typeCollateral role type distiguishes the Collateral role according to the role the Collateral acts in.
8Investor_identifierAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
9Investor_role_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
10Long_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_Security_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
12Allocated_unused_collateral_valueAllocated unused collateral value is an amount that expresses the protection value that is not used for risk mitigation and, for FINREP reporting purposes is allocated to a specific Loan and advance, which is the Long non-negotiable security position.
13Protection_allocated_valueProtection allocated value is the maximum amount of the protection value, that can be considered as credit protection for the Long non-negotiable security position. For protections that is eligible under Regulation (EU) No 575/2013, this value should be reported in accordance with Part 2 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
14Allocated_collateral_value_uncapped_pre_haircutsAllocated collateral value uncapped pre haircuts is an amount that is the value of the collateral that is not used for risk mitigations (uncapped) and before any haircuts have been applied, and that is for FINREP reporting "allocated" as unused amount to the Debt security.
Indexes
Index NameStateFunctionalSpatialExpressionColumn NameSort
Order

Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear

Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
4Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
5Protection_arrangement_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Protection_arrangement_role_typePFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Investor_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8Investor_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
9Security_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
10Security_position_Balance_sheet_recognised_typePFYStringDOMSecurity position Balance sheet recognised type (Input Layer)
11Long/Short_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_typePFYStringDOMLong/Short balance sheet recognised security position type (Input Layer)
12Allocated_unused_collateral_valueYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
13Protection_allocated_valueYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Protection_arrangement_identifierAn identifier to univocally identify each protection arragement.
6Protection_arrangement_role_typeProtection arrangement role type distinguishes the Protection arrangement role according to their nature.
7Investor_identifierAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
8Investor_role_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
9Security_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
12Allocated_unused_collateral_valueAllocated unused collateral value is an amount that expresses the protection value that is not used for risk mitigation and, for FINREP reporting purposes is allocated to a specific Loan and advance, which is the Long non-negotiable security position.
13Protection_allocated_valueProtection allocated value is the maximum amount of the protection value, via the Protection arrangement, that can be considered as credit protection for the Long security position. For protections that is eligible under Regulation (EU) No 575/2013, this value should be reported in accordance with Part 2 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.

Expected Growth0

NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation

10Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
