36Transfer_between_impairment_stagesYStringDOMTransfers between impairment stages (Input Layer)
37Source_of_encumbranceYString (2) DOMSources of encumbrance - Input Layer
38Accrued_interestDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
39Accumulated_changes_in_fair_valueDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
40Accumulated_changes_in_fair_value_due_to_credit_riskDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
41Encumbered_number_of_sharesDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
42General_allowances_for_bank_risk_affecting_carrying_amount_(nGAAP)DECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals

44Haircuts_for_trading_position_valued_at_fair_valueDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
45Nominal_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
46Number_of_sharesDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
47Percentage_retainedDECIMALDOMReal numbers from 0 to 1 with 6 decimals
48Current_loan-to-value_ratioDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
49Accumulated_partial_write-offsDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
50Accumulated_total_write-offsDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
51Gross_carrying_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
52Accumulated_coverage_ratioDECIMALDOMReal numbers from 0 to 1 with 6 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Accounting_classificationAccounting portfolio where the asset or liability is recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP –under Regulation (EU) 2015/534 (ECB/2015/13) applied by the observed agent's legal entity.
2Prudential_portfolio_typeA discriminator to distinguish between trading and banking (or non-trading) book.
5Investor_identifierAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
6Investor_role_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
7Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
8Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
9Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
11Security_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
12Accumulated_impairmentThe amount of loss allowances that are held against or are allocated to the asset on the reporting reference date. This data attribute applies to assets subject to impairment under the applied accounting standard. Under IFRS, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected
credit losses;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected
credit losses.
Under GAAP, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to general allowances;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to specific allowances.
13Accumulated_write-offsCumulative amount of principal and past due interest of any debt instrument that the institution is no longer recognising because they are considered uncollectible, independently of the portfolio in which they were included. Write-offs could be caused both by reductions in the carrying amount of financial assets recognised directly in profit or loss and by reductions in the amounts of the allowance accounts for credit losses set off against the carrying amount of financial assets.
14Arrears_for_the_instrumentAggregate amount of principal, interest and any fee payment outstanding at the reporting date, which is contractually due and has not been paid (past due). This amount is always to be reported. '0' is to be reported if the instrument was not past due on the reporting date.
15Available_for_encumbrance_indicatorAvailable for encumbrance indicator distinguishes the asset into being available for asset encumbrance and not being available for asset encumbrance.
16Carrying_amountThe carrying amount in accordance with Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
17Credit_risk_induced_accumulated_negative_value_adjustmentsAccumulated negative value adjustments. Market risk induced
18Encumbered_asset_indicatorEncumbered asset indicator signals whether the asset is encumbered or not.
19Fair_valueFair value as defined in IFRS 13.9.
20Fair_value_changeFair value changes in the period and before taxes.
21Fair_value_changes_due_to_hedge_accountingChanges in the fair value of an asset or liability, which is a hedged item and measured at amortised cost, that are recognised in the carrying amount due to the application of hedge accounting (IFRS 9.6)
22Fair_value_hierarchyLevel of fair value of a financial asset or liability according to the hierarchy defined in IFRS 13. Applicable to national GAAP based on BAD only if the allocation of instruments is measured at fair value according to different levels.
24Gross_carrying_amount_excluding_accrued_interestGross carrying amount, as defined in IFRS 9 appendix A, excluding accrued interest
25Impairment_assessment_methodThe method by which the impairment is assessed, if the instrument is subject to impairment in accordance with applied accounting standards. Collective and individual methods are distinguished.
26Impairment_statusType of impairment recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP.
27Initial_impairment_statusInitial imapirment status is the impairment status, recorded in the beginning of the financial year or at the initial recognition of the impairment of the exposure after the beginning of the financial year.
28Litigation_statusFinrep Annex V,  Part 2.321, 322: An exposure shall be ‘in pre-litigation status’ where the debtor has been formally notified that the institution will take legal action against the debtor within a defined time period, unless certain contractual or other payment obligations are met. That shall also include cases where the contract has been terminated by the reporting institution because the debtor is in formal breach of the terms and conditions of the contract and the debtor has been notified accordingly, but no legal action against the debtor has formally been taken by the institution yet. Exposures classified as ‘in pre-litigation status’ can exit this classification if the outstanding amounts are paid or if they enter into litigation status as defined in the following paragraph. 
An exposure shall be ‘in litigation status’ where legal action against the debtor has formally been taken. This comprises cases where a court of law confirmed that formal judiciary proceedings have occurred or the judiciary system has been notified of the intention to commence legal proceedings.
30Market_risk_induced_accumulated_negative_value_adjustmentsAccumulated negative value adjustments. Market risk induced
31Non-performing_prior_to_forbearance_indicatorNon-performing prior to forbearance specifies if an asset is classified as non-performing prior to forbearance according to Regulation (EU) 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.263.
32Retained_debt_security_indicatorRetained security indicator distinguishes the Long debt security position derived data into those positions that have been retained by the reporting agent and those that are not retained. 

The Long security debt position is retained if the investor is the reporting agent.
33Subject_to_impairment_indicatorSubject to impairment indicator indicates whether the asset is subject to impairment or not.
34Derived_default_statusDerived default status is the default status where all specific statusses of default for the Long debt security position derived data have been mapped to either "Default" or "Not in default".
35Long_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_prudential_portfolio_Accounting_classification_assignment_typeLong balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classifcation assignment type defines whether Long balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classifcation assignment is a debt secuirtyo r equity or fund.
36Transfer_between_impairment_stagesTransfer between impairment stages compares the impairment status at the beginning of the period (Initial impairment status) with the impairment status at the end of the period.
37Source_of_encumbranceType of transaction in which the exposure is encumbered in accordance with Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014. An asset will be treated as encumbered if it has been pledged or if it is subject to any form of arrangement to secure, collateralise or credit enhance any instrument from which it cannot be freely withdrawn.
38Accrued_interestThe amount of accrued interest on loans at the reporting reference date as defined in Regulation (EU) No 1071/2013 (ECB/2013/33). In accordance with the general principle of accruals accounting, interest receivable on instruments should be subject to on-balance sheet recording as it accrues (i.e. on an accruals basis) rather than when it is actually received (i.e. on a cash basis).
39Accumulated_changes_in_fair_valueAmount of gains and losses from re-measurement of the asset accumulated from the initial recognition to the reference date.
40Accumulated_changes_in_fair_value_due_to_credit_riskAccumulated changes in fair value due to credit risk in accordance with Part 2.46 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
41Encumbered_number_of_sharesThe number of shares that are encumbered according to ITS on Asset encumbrance, 1.7 Definition of encumbrance.
42General_allowances_for_bank_risk_affecting_carrying_amount_(nGAAP)General allowances for bank risk affecting carrying amount (nGAAP)
43General_allowances_for_credit_risk_affecting_carrying_amountGeneral allowances for credit risk affecting carrying amount.
44Haircuts_for_trading_position_valued_at_fair_valueHaircut for trading position valued at fair value. 
According to Annex V (5.29):
Where applicable under national GAAP based on BAD, ‘Haircuts for trading positions valued at fair value’ shall be reported. The haircuts decrease the value of trading assets and increase the value of trading liabilities.
45Nominal_amountHolding amount at nominal value
46Number_of_sharesThe number of shares of a security.
47Percentage_retainedPercentage retained is a percentage that states how much of the Long debt security positions nominal value is part of the Debt security issued (by the reporting agent) nominal value.
48Current_loan-to-value_ratioCurrent loan-to-value ratio (LTV-C) means the sum of all non-negotiable security positions that are loans or loan tranches secured by the borrower on a property at the reporting date relative to the current value of the property (ESRB), allocated per Long non-negotiable security position.
49Accumulated_partial_write-offsAccording to FinRep Annex 5: "the accumulated partial (...) amount as at the reference date of principal and accrued past due interest and fees of any debt instrument that has been de-recognised to date (...) because the institution has no reasonable expectations of recovering the contractual cash flows."
50Accumulated_total_write-offsAccording to FinRep Annex 5: "the accumulated (...) total amount as at the reference date of principal and accrued past due interest and fees of any debt instrument that has been de-recognised to date (...) because the institution has no reasonable expectations of recovering the contractual cash flows."

51Gross_carrying_amountGross carrying amount as defined in Annex V 5.2.
52Accumulated_coverage_ratioAccumulated coverage ratio is calculated as the ratio between the accumulated impairments, respectively the accumulated negative changes in fair value due to credit risk related to a loan or advance as numerator, and the gross carrying amount of that loan or advance as denominator.

10 nGAAP: Loans and receivables

3nGAAP: Trading Financial assets: Trading financial assets in accordance with national GAAP.
4IFRS: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial assets measured at fair value through profit and loss and designated as such upon initial recognition or subsequently in accordance with IFRS, except those classified as financial assets held for trading.
41IFRS: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with IFRS.
45nGAAP: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits in accordance with national GAAP.
46nGAAP: Financial assets held for trading

6IFRS: Financial assets at amortised cost: Financial assets measured at amortised cost in accordance with IFRS.
64nGAAP: financial assets at fair value or strict LOCOM
7nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value through profit or loss: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity in accordance with national GAAP.
711Accounting portfolios for financial assets other than classified as held for sale, excluding financial assets held for trading, trading financial assets and cash and cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits
73nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets. LOCOM: nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets at LOCOM
74nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets. Other than LOCOM
76nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method. LOCOM: nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method at LOCOM
77nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method. Other than LOCOM
8IFRS: Financial assets at fair value through other comprehensive income: Financial assets measured at fair value through other comprehensive income due to business model and cash-flows characteristics in accordance with IFRS.
83Investments in subsidiaries, joint ventures and associates.
851nGAAP: Accounting portfolios for trading financial assetss. Cost based method or LOCOM
9nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity in accordance with national GAAP.

Values List
ValueDescription
1Not encumbered asset
100Encumbered asset
Fair_value_hierarchy
Values List
ValueDescription
1Level 1: Level 1 inputs used for the measurement of fair value. Level 1 inputs are quoted prices in active markets for identical assets or liabilities that the entity can access at the measurement date
2Level 2: Level 2 inputs used for the measurment of fair value. Level 2 inputs are inputs other than quoted prices included within Level 1 that are observable for the asset or liability, either directly or indirectly
3Level 3: Level 3 inputs used for the measurment of fair value. Level 3 inputs are unobservable inputs for the asset or liability
Fair_value_option_designation
Values List
ValueDescription
1Accounting mismatch
2Management on a fair value basis
3Management of credit risk. Upon designation
4Management of credit risk. After the designation
Impairment_assessment_method
Values List
ValueDescription
1Collectively assessed
2Individually assessed
Impairment_status
Values List
ValueDescription
211General allowances for credit risk (GAAP)
212General allowances for banking risk (GAAP)
23Stage 1 (IFRS): To be used if the instrument is not impaired and a loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected credit losses is raised against the instrument under IFRS. Only for instruments subject to impairment under IFRS 9.
24Stage 2 (IFRS): To be used if the instrument is not impaired and a loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected credit losses is raised against the instrument under IFRS. Only for instruments subject to impairment under IFRS 9.
25Stage 3 (IFRS): To be used if the instrument is impaired and a loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected credit losses is raised against the instrument under IFRS. Only for instruments subject to impairment under IFRS 9.
26Specific allowances (GAAP): To be used if the instrument is subject to impairment in accordance with an applied accounting standard other than IFRS 9 and specific loss allowances are raised, irrespective of whether these allowances are individually or collectively assessed (impaired
27Purchased or originated credit-impaired instruments (POCI) (IFRS)
Initial_impairment_status
Values List
ValueDescription
23Stage 1 (IFRS)
24Stage 2 (IFRS)
25Stage 3 (IFRS)
Litigation_status
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1In pre-litigation
2In litigation
3Not in litigation/pre-litigation

Source_of_encumbrance
Values List
ValueDescription
10Debt securities issued - covered bonds securities: Covered bonds securities issued in accordance with the EBA’s implementing technical standards on asset encumbrance reporting as referred to in Article 99(5) and Article 100 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.
11Debt securities issued - asset-backed securities: Asset-backed securities (ABS) issued in accordance with the EBA’s implementing technical standards on asset encumbrance reporting as referred to in Article 99(5) and Article 100 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.
12Debt securities issued - other than covered bonds and ABSs: Debt securities issued other than covered bonds and ABSs in accordance with the EBA’s implementing technical standards on asset encumbrance reporting as referred to in Article 99(5) and Article 100 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.
13Other sources of encumbrance: Other sources of encumbrance in accordance with the EBA’s implementing technical standards on asset encumbrance reporting as referred to in Article 99(5) and Article 100 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.
14Repurchase agreements
15Short position
16Loan commitments received
17Financial guarantees received
18Securities borrowed with non-cash collateral
6Exchange traded derivatives: Exchange traded derivatives in accordance with the EBA’s implementing technical standards on asset encumbrance reporting as referred to in Article 99(5) and Article 100 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.
7Over-the-counter derivatives: Over-the-counter derivatives in accordance with the EBA’s implementing technical standards on asset encumbrance reporting as referred to in Article 99(5) and Article 100 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.
9Deposits other than repurchase agreements: Deposits other than repurchase agreements in accordance with the EBA’s implementing technical standards on asset encumbrance reporting as referred to in Article 99(5) and Article 100 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.

Table NameLong_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_prudential_portfolio_Accounting_classification_assignment_national_general_accepted_accounting_principles_(nGAAP)_fiduciary_item
Functional NameLong balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP) fiduciary item
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionLong balance sheet recognised security position  prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP) fiduciary asset is a Long balance sheet recognised security position  prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment that is a fiduciary asset

Notes
Number Of Columns12
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Accounting_classificationPFYString (3) DOMAccounting classification for assets
2Prudential_portfolio_typePFYString (1) DOMPrudential portfolio
3Long/Short_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_typePFYStringDOMLong/Short balance sheet recognised security position type (Input Layer)
4Security_position_Balance_sheet_recognised_typePFYStringDOMSecurity position Balance sheet recognised type (Input Layer)
5Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
7Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
8Investor_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
9Investor_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
10Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
11Security_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
12Fiduciary_asset_indicatorYString (1) DOMFiduciary asset indicator
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Accounting_classificationAccounting portfolio where the asset or liability is recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP –under Regulation (EU) 2015/534 (ECB/2015/13) applied by the observed agent's legal entity.
2Prudential_portfolio_typeA discriminator to distinguish between trading and banking (or non-trading) book.
5Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
6Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
8Investor_identifierAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
9Investor_role_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
10Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
11Security_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
12Fiduciary_asset_indicatorIdentification of instruments in which the observed agent acts in its own name but on behalf of and with the risk borne by a third party.

Table NameLong_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_prudential_portfolio_Accounting_classification_assignment_taken_into_possession
Functional NameLong balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment taken into possession
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Long balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment taken into possession is a  Long balance sheet recognised security position that was not pledged by the debtor as collateral, but was obtained in exchange for the cancellation of debt, whether on a voluntary basis or as part of legal proceeding
Notes
Number Of Columns18
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Accounting_classificationPFYString (3) DOMAccounting classification for assets
2Prudential_portfolio_typePFYString (1) DOMPrudential portfolio
3Long/Short_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_typePFYStringDOMLong/Short balance sheet recognised security position type (Input Layer)
4Security_position_Balance_sheet_recognised_typePFYStringDOMSecurity position Balance sheet recognised type (Input Layer)
5Reference_datePFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
7Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
8Accounting_standardPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
9Investor_identifierPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
10Investor_role_typePFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
11Long_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_Security_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
12Accumulated_fair_value_changes_of_the_exposure_due_to_credit_riskYDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
13Balance_sheet_recognition_dateYDateDOMAll possible dates
14Derecognised_exposure_accumulated_impairmentYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
15Derecognised_exposure_gross_carrying_amountYDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
16Gross_carrying_amount_at_initial_recognitionYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
17Accumulated_negative_changesYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
18Time_since_initial_recognitionYString (2) DOMTime elapsed since initial recognition band
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Accounting_classificationAccounting portfolio where the asset or liability is recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP –under Regulation (EU) 2015/534 (ECB/2015/13) applied by the observed agent's legal entity.
2Prudential_portfolio_typeA discriminator to distinguish between trading and banking (or non-trading) book.
5Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
6Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
7Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
9Investor_identifierAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
10Investor_role_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
11Long_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_Security_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
12Accumulated_fair_value_changes_of_the_exposure_due_to_credit_riskAccumulated changes in fair value due to credit risk in accordance with Part 2.46 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014 of the exposure that is derecognised because of the asset obtained by taking possession.

Note: This amount must only be reported when it is negative.
13Balance_sheet_recognition_dateBalance sheet recognition date is a date on the asset obtained by taking possession  was taken into possession and thus recognised on the balance sheet.
14Derecognised_exposure_accumulated_impairmentAccumulated impairment is the amount of loss allowances that are held against or are allocated to the derecognised exposure on the reporting reference date for which the asset obtained by taking possession has been obtained.

This data attribute applies to assets subject to impairment under the applied accounting standard. Under IFRS, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected
credit losses;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected
credit losses.
Under GAAP, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to general allowances;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to specific allowances.
16Gross_carrying_amount_at_initial_recognitionGross carrying amount at the point in time of the initial recognition in the balance sheet 
17Accumulated_negative_changesAccumulated negative changes shall be the difference, at the level of the individual asset obtained by taking in to possession  item, between the value at initial recognition of the asset  and the carrying amount at the reporting reference date, where that difference is negative. (FINREP Annex V)
18Time_since_initial_recognitionTime elapsed since initial recognition band classifies time elapsed since initial recognition in the balance sheet into bands used for FINREP reporting.
Indexes
Index NameStateFunctionalSpatialExpressionColumn NameSort
Order

Column LevelAccounting_classification
Values List
ValueDescription
10 nGAAP: Loans and receivables
12nGAAP: Held-to-maturity investments
14IFRS: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits in accordance with IFRS.
2IFRS: Financial assets held for trading: Financial assets held for trading in accordance with IFRS.
3nGAAP: Trading Financial assets: Trading financial assets in accordance with national GAAP.
4IFRS: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial assets measured at fair value through profit and loss and designated as such upon initial recognition or subsequently in accordance with IFRS, except those classified as financial assets held for trading.
41IFRS: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with IFRS.
45nGAAP: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits in accordance with national GAAP.
46nGAAP: Financial assets held for trading
47nGAAP: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with national GAAP.
48nGAAP: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss
5nGAAP: Available-for-sale financial assets
6IFRS: Financial assets at amortised cost: Financial assets measured at amortised cost in accordance with IFRS.
64nGAAP: financial assets at fair value or strict LOCOM
7nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value through profit or loss: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity in accordance with national GAAP.
711Accounting portfolios for financial assets other than classified as held for sale, excluding financial assets held for trading, trading financial assets and cash and cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits
73nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets. LOCOM: nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets at LOCOM
74nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets. Other than LOCOM
76nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method. LOCOM: nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method at LOCOM
77nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method. Other than LOCOM
8IFRS: Financial assets at fair value through other comprehensive income: Financial assets measured at fair value through other comprehensive income due to business model and cash-flows characteristics in accordance with IFRS.
83Investments in subsidiaries, joint ventures and associates.
851nGAAP: Accounting portfolios for trading financial assetss. Cost based method or LOCOM
9nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity in accordance with national GAAP.

Functional NameLong balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio assignment risk data
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Long balance sheet recognised security position risk data is risk related information about a Long balance sheet recognised debt security position.
Notes
Number Of Columns17
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Exposure_classPYString (2) DOMExposure classes for the calculation of risk weight
2Prudential_portfolio_typePFYString (1) DOMPrudential portfolio
3Long/Short_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_typePFYStringDOMLong/Short balance sheet recognised security position type (Input Layer)
4Security_position_Balance_sheet_recognised_typePFYStringDOMSecurity position Balance sheet recognised type (Input Layer)
5Investor_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Investor_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
7Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
8Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
9Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
10Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
11Security_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
12Approach_for_prudential_purposesYString (3) DOMApproach for prudential purposes
13Exposure_valueYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
14Loss_given_default_(LGD)_in_downturnsYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
15Loss_given_default_(LGD)_in_normal_economic_timesYDECIMALDOMNon-negative real numbers with 2 decimals
16Risk-weightYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
17Specific_risk_weightYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Exposure_classExposure class according to Regulation (EU) 575/2013, Article 112 Exposure class.
2Prudential_portfolio_typeA discriminator to distinguish between trading and banking (or non-trading) book.
5Investor_identifierAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
6Investor_role_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
7Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
8Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
9Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
11Security_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
12Approach_for_prudential_purposesIn accordance with Article 107 of the CRR, Approach to which Standardized Approach provided for in Chapter 2 or, if permitted by the competent authorities in accordance with Article 143, the Internal Ratings Based Approach provided for in Chapter 3 to calculate their risk-weighted exposure amounts for the purposes of points (a) and (f) of Article 92(3).
13Exposure_valueExposure Value according to Regulation (EU) 575/2013, Article 111 Exposure value.
14Loss_given_default_(LGD)_in_downturnsLoss given default (LGD) in downturns
15Loss_given_default_(LGD)_in_normal_economic_timesLoss given default (LGD) in normal economic times
16Risk-weightRisk-weight
17Specific_risk_weightSpecific risk weight.

Values List
ValueDescription
1SA: Equity exposures
10SA: Exposures to international organisations
11SA: Exposures to multilateral development banks
12SA: Exposures to public sector entities
13SA: Exposures to regional governments or local authorities
14SA: Items associated with a particular high risk
16SA: Other items
2SA: Exposures in default
3SA: Exposures in the form of covered bonds
4SA: Exposures in the form of units or shares in CIUs
6SA: Exposures to central governments or central banks
7SA: Exposures to corporates without a short-term credit assessment
8SA: Exposures to institutions and corporates with a short-term credit assessment
9SA: Exposures to institutions without a short-term credit assessment

1Trading book
2Non-trading book

Accounting_consolidation_level
Values List

0Not applicable
1Solo consolidation level
2Group consolidation level
Accounting_standard
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1National GAAP not consistent with IFRS
2IFRS
3National GAAP consistent with IFRS
Approach_for_prudential_purposes
Values List
ValueDescription
11250% for positions not subject to any method: Securitisations: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for securitisation positions to which a risk weight of 1250% is assigned in accordance with Article 259(1)(d) of CRR.
118SEC-IRBA: Internal ratings-based approach for securitisation positions: Securitisations: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for securitisation positions for which the Internal ratings-based approach is used.
122SEC-SA: Standardised approach for securitisations: Securitisations: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for securitisation positions for which the Standardised approach is used.
125SEC-ERBA: External ratings-based approach for securitisation positions: Securitisations: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for securitisation positions for which the External ratings-based approach is used
18Ratings based method: Securitisations: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for securitisation positions for which the Ratings based method set out in Article 261 of CRR is used.
19Supervisory formula method: Securitisations: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for securitisation positions for which the Supervisory formula method set out in Article 262 of CRR is used.
25Internal assessment approach: Securitisations: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for securitisation positions for which the Internal assessment approach set out in Article 259(3) and (4) of CRR is used.
30Look-through approach: Securitisations: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for securitisation positions for which the Look-through approach set out in Article 259(1)(e) of CRR is used.
33IRB: Internal models approach: Equity exposures: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for equity exposures for which the Internal models approach set out in Article 155(4) of CRR is used.
34IRB: PD/LGD approach: Equity exposures: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for equity exposures for which the PD/LGD approach set out in Article 155(3) of CRR is used.
35IRB: Simple risk weight approach: Equity exposures: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for equity exposures for which the Simple risk weight approach set out in Article 155(2) of CRR is used.
42Standardised approach: Standardised approach to calculate the risk-weighted exposure amounts in accordance with Chapter 2 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013
66Advanced IRB: Internal Ratings Based (IRB) approach to calculate the risk-weighted exposure amounts in accordance with Chapter 3 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013 and banks calculate and use, in addition to the PDs, their own risk parameter subject to the supervisory guid
67Foundation IRB: Internal Ratings Based (IRB) approach to calculate the risk-weighted exposure amounts in accordance with Chapter 3 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013 and banks use their own-calculated PDs, while for the other parameters (such as LGD) predefined values provid
68IRB: Fixed risk weights: Equity exposures: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for equity exposures which attract a fixed risk weight treatment (without however being explicitly treated according to the Simple Risk Weight approach or the (temporary or permanent) partial use of the credit risk standardised approach) set out in Article 155 of CRR.
Loss_given_default_(LGD)_in_downturns

0

Table NameLong_non-balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_collateral_received_assigment
Functional NameLong non-balance sheet recognised security position collateral received assigment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionLong non-balance sheet recognised security position collateral received assigment is the combination of Long non- balance sheet recognised security position and collateral received stating which Long non- balance sheet recognised security position is protected by which Collateral.
Notes
Number Of Columns13
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999

Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
4Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
5Collateral_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Collateral_role_typePFYString (1) DOMCollateral role type (Input layer)
7Investor_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8Investor_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
9Security_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
10Security_position_Balance_sheet_recognised_typePFYStringDOMSecurity position Balance sheet recognised type (Input Layer)
11Allocated_unused_collateral_valueYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
12Protection_allocated_valueYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
13Allocated_collateral_value_uncapped_pre_haircutsDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Collateral_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each collateral.
6Collateral_role_typeCollateral role type distiguishes the Collateral role according to the role the Collateral acts in.
7Investor_identifierAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
8Investor_role_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
9Security_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
11Allocated_unused_collateral_valueAllocated unused collateral value is an amount that expresses the protection value that is not used for risk mitigation and, for FINREP reporting purposes is allocated to a specific Loan and advance, which is the Long non-negotiable security position.
12Protection_allocated_valueProtection allocated value is the maximum amount of the protection value, that can be considered as credit protection for the Long non-negotiable security position. For protections that is eligible under Regulation (EU) No 575/2013, this value should be reported in accordance with Part 2 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
13Allocated_collateral_value_uncapped_pre_haircutsAllocated collateral value uncapped pre haircuts is an amount that is the value of the collateral that is not used for risk mitigations (uncapped) and before any haircuts have been applied, and that is for FINREP reporting "allocated" as unused amount to the Debt security.
Indexes

Order

Classification Type Name

MV Prebuilt
MV Query

NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation

2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore

4Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
5Protection_arrangement_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore

Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Protection_arrangement_identifierAn identifier to univocally identify each protection arragement.
6Protection_arrangement_role_typeProtection arrangement role type distinguishes the Protection arrangement role according to their nature.
7Investor_identifierAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
8Investor_role_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
9Security_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
11Allocated_unused_collateral_valueAllocated unused collateral value is an amount that expresses the protection value that is not used for risk mitigation and, for FINREP reporting purposes is allocated to a specific Loan and advance, which is the Long non-negotiable security position.
12Protection_allocated_valueProtection allocated value is the maximum amount of the protection value, via the Protection arrangement, that can be considered as credit protection for the Long security position. For protections that is eligible under Regulation (EU) No 575/2013, this value should be reported in accordance with Part 2 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.

Table NameNatural_person_Key_management_personnel_assignment
Functional NameNatural person Key management personnel assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionNatural person Key management personnel assignment is the combination of Key management personnel and Natural person that indicates which Natural person directly or indirectly influences the Key management personnel.

 If an individual is related to the entity based on the criteria stated above, all their close family members are also considered related parties. Close family members are defined as family members who are likely to influence, or be influenced by, that individual in their interactions with the entity (IAS 24.9). (via https://ifrscommunity.com/knowledge-base/ias-24-related-party-disclosures/#definition-of-a-related-party)
Notes
Number Of Columns7
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Key_management_personnel_party_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
3Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Natural_personl_group_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
5Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
6Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
7Natural_person_party_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Key_management_personnel_party_identifierNatural person is an identifier that uniquely identifies the natural person involved in the Natural person Key management personnel assignement.
2Reference_dateKey management personnel Party reference date is the date the data of the Key management personnel  refers to.
3Reporting_agent_identifierNatural person group role reporting agent identifier identifies the reporting agent that reports on the Natural person group role.
4Natural_personl_group_role_typeNatural person group role type indicates the role that the natural person has for the group.
5Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
7Natural_person_party_identifierNatural person is an identifier that uniquely identifies the natural person involved in the Natural person Key management personnel assignement.

2Investor_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
3Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
4Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
6Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
7Security_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8Security_position_Balance_sheet_recognised_typePFYStringDOMSecurity position Balance sheet recognised type (Input Layer)
9Fiduciary_asset_indicatorYString (1) DOMFiduciary asset indicator
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Investor_identifierAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
2Investor_role_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
3Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
4Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
5Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
7Security_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
9Fiduciary_asset_indicatorIdentification of instruments in which the observed agent acts in its own name but on behalf of and with the risk borne by a third party.

Table NameNon-financial_asset
Functional NameNon-financial asset
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Non-financial asset is an Asset that is not a Financial asset.
Notes
Number Of Columns11
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Non-financial_asset_IDPYString (120)  - AI - IDDOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2Accounting_standardPYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
4Held_for_sale_indicatorPYString (1) DOMHeld for sale indicator (Input Layer)
5Reference_datePYDateDOMAll possible dates
6Reporting_agent_identifierPYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Carrying_amountYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
8Measurement_methodYStringDOMMeasurement method (Input Layer)
9Non-financial_asset_typeYString (4) DOMType of non-financial asset (Input Layer)
10Software_asset_indicatorYString (1) DOMSoftware assets indicator (Input Layer)
11Subject_to_operating_lease_indicatorYString (1) DOMSubject to operating lease Indicator (Input Layer)
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
4Held_for_sale_indicatorHeld for sale  indicates the Non-current assets and instruments in disposal groups classified as held for sale in accordance with IFRS5. Is not applicable under national GAAP based on BAD.
5Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
6Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
7Carrying_amountThe carrying amount in accordance with Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
8Measurement_methodMeasurement method identifies the type of measurement method according to IAS (See IAS 16.30, 16.31 and IAS 40.33-55, 76). It distinguishes into their cost or revaluation model. 
9Non-financial_asset_typeA discriminator to distinguish between non-financial assets and non-financial liabilities.
10Software_asset_indicatorTime elapsed since initial recognition band classifies time elapsed since initial recognition in the balance sheet into bands used for FINREP reporting.
11Subject_to_operating_lease_indicatorAn indicator to specify if an object is subject to operating lease or not.

Table NameOther_party_identifier
Functional NameOther party identifier
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionOther party identifier is a code (different to the attribute Party identifier) of a Party that identifies the party in systems outside of the bank. It is a combination of a Party and a Party  identifier type.

Number Of Columns8
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear

kind		Domain NameFormula

Table NameOver_the_counter_(OTC)_Derivative_as_a_hedge
Functional NameOver the counter (OTC) Derivative as a hedge
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionAn Over the counter (OTC) Derivative as a hedge is an Over the counter (OTC) Derivative role where the Over the counter (OTC) Derivative instrument acts as a hedge.
Notes
Number Of Columns9
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Accounting_standardPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
5Instrument_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Accounting_hedge_indicatorYString (1) DOMAccounting hedge indicator
7At_cost-based_method_or_Lower_of_costs_or_market_(LOCOM)_indicatorYString (1) DOMHedge accounting - At cost based method or Lower of cost or market (LOCOM) indicator (Input layer)
8Over_the_counter_(OTC)_Derivative_hedge_accounting_standard_typeYString (1) DOMOver the counter (OTC) Derivative hedge accounting standard type
9Type_of_hedgeYString (2) DOMType of hedge
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Instrument_identifierAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
6Accounting_hedge_indicatorAn indicator specifying if the hedge is considered an accounting hedge or not.
7At_cost-based_method_or_Lower_of_costs_or_market_(LOCOM)_indicatorAt cost based method or Lower of costs or market (LOCOM) indicator distinguishes the used accounting portfolio for national General Accepted Accounting Principles (nGAAP) between those that use the At cost based method or Lower of costs or market (LOCOM), and those that do not use At cost based method or Lower of costs or carket (LOCOM).
8Over_the_counter_(OTC)_Derivative_hedge_accounting_standard_typeOver the counter (OTC) Derivative hedge accounting standard type distinguishes the Over the counter (OTC) Derivative as a hedge according to the applied accounting standard.
9Type_of_hedgeType of hedge applicable under the relevant accounting standard.

At_cost-based_method_or_Lower_of_costs_or_market_(LOCOM)_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1At cost based method or Lower of cost or market (LOCOM)
2Not at cost based method or Lower of cost or market(LOCOM)
Over_the_counter_(OTC)_Derivative_hedge_accounting_standard_type
Values List
ValueDescription
1Over the counter (OTC) Derivative as a hedge according to International Financial Reporting Standard (IFRS)
2Over the counter (OTC) Derivative as a hedge according to national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP)
Type_of_hedge
Values List
ValueDescription
1Fair value hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (a)
11Hedges other than cash flow hedge and hedge of net investment in a foreign operation: Economic hedge
12Economic hedge. With use of fair value option
13Economic hedge. Other
2Cash flow hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (b)
3Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (c)
4Portfolio fair value hedges of interest rate risk: Hedging relationship as defined in IAS39.AG 114-132
5Portfolio cash flow hedges of interest rate risk: Hedging relationship as defined in IAS39.AG 114-132
6Cost-price hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.114. This hedging relationship is applicable only under nGAAP based on BAD
7Other than Fair value hedge, Cash flow hedge, Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation, Portfolio fair value hedges of interest rate risk, Portfolio cash flow hedges of interest rate risk, Cost-price hedge: Other applicable hedging relationship for nGAAP based on BAD reporters

Table NamePartner_enterprise_assignment
Functional NamePartner enterprise assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionPartner enterprise assignment is the combination of a Central bank and private sector company and a Parent enterprise that indicates which Central bank and private sector company is a partner to which Central bank and private sector company in the sense of Commission Recommendation 2003/361/EC.
Notes
Number Of Columns8
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3Central_bank_and_private_sector_company_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
5Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
6Partner_enterprise_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
7Partner_enterprise_party_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8Percentage_interest_in_the_capital_or_voting_rightsYDECIMALDOMReal numbers from 0 to 1 with 6 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Central_bank_and_private_sector_company_identifierThe party identifier of the involved partner enterprise.
4Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
6Partner_enterprise_role_typePartner enterprise Party role type is the role type identifying the central bank and private sector company role type as Partner enterprise in the Partner enterprise assignment.
7Partner_enterprise_party_identifierThe party identifier of the involved partner enterprise.
8Percentage_interest_in_the_capital_or_voting_rightsPercentage interest in the capital or voting rights (whichever is greater).

Table NameParty
Functional NameParty risk data
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Party is an entity of interest.
Notes
Number Of Columns48
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
4Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
5Party_identifierPYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Institutional_unit_group_identifierFString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Ultimate_parent_undertaking_organisation_identifierFString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8Organisation_identifierFString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
9Area_where_the_entity_is_located_according_to_SHSYString (5) DOMArea where the entity is located
10Controlled_by_national_or_foreign_bodiesYString (1) DOMControlled by national or foreign bodies (Input Layer)
11Country_codeYString (2) DOMISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code of the country (Input Layer)
12Date_of_default_statusYDateDOMAll possible dates
13Default_statusYString (2) DOMDefault status type
14Enterprise_indicatorYString (1) DOMEnterprise indicator (Input Layer)
15Enterprise_sizeYString (1) DOMEnterprise size
16Enterprise_size_dateYDateDOMAll possible dates
17Governed_Country_codeYString (2) DOMISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code of the country
18Institutional_sectorYString (8) DOMInstitutional sectors
19International_organisation_codeYString (3) DOMPseudo-ISO code for international organisations of the Eurostat’s ‘Balance of Payments Vademecum’ (Input Layer)
20Leading_branch_for_AnaCredit_reporting_indicatorYString (2) DOMLeading branch indicator (Input Layer)
21Legal_formYString (9) DOMLegal form (Input Layer)
22Legal_proceeding_statusYString (1) DOMLegal proceedings status (Input Layer)
23Listed_central_bank_and_private_sector_company_indicatorYStringDOMListed central bank and private sector company indicator (Input Layer)
24Market_capitalisation_indicatorYString (1) DOMMarket Capitalisation indicator (Input Layer)
25Multilateral_development_bank_indicatorYString (1) DOMMultilateral development bank indicator (Input Layer)
26Nomenclature_des_unités_territoriales_statistiques_(NUTS)_regionYString (5) DOMNUTS 3 codes (Input Layer)
27Organisation_type_by_legal_proceeding_statusYString (2) DOMOrganisation type by legal proceeding status (Input Layer)
28Party_typeYString (6) DOMType of Party (Input Layer)
29Party_type_by_addressYString (3) DOMParty type by address (Input Layer)
30Performing_statusYString (2) DOMPerforming status
31Performing_status_reasonYString (1) DOMPerforming status reason
32Pulling_effect_indicatorYString (1) DOMPulling effect indicator (Input Layer)
33Statistical_Classification_of_Economic_Activities_in_the_European_Community_(NACE)YString (5) DOMNACE levels 2 to 4 plus not applicable (staging)
34Statistical_Classification_of_Economic_Activities_in_the_European_Community_revision_(NACE_2_1)YString (5) DOMEconomic Activity V2
35Type_of_economyYString (1) DOMType of economy (Input Layer)
36Observed_agent_indicator_for_Anacredit_reportingYString (1) DOMObserved agent indicator for Anacredit reporting
37Annual_turnoverDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
38Balance_sheet_totalDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
39City_/_town_/_villageString (250) DOMString-ASCII up to 250 characters
40Date_of_initiation_of_legal_proceedingDateDOMAll possible dates
41First_nameString (50) DOMString up to 50 characters
42Last_nameString (50) DOMString up to 50 characters
43Legal_entity_identifier_(LEI)String (20) DOMString with 18 characters (capital letters or digits) followed by 2 digits
44Number_of_employeesREALDOMNon-negative real numbers with 0 decimals
45Organisation_nameString (250) DOMString-ASCII up to 250 characters
46Postal_codeString (255) DOMString up to 255 characters - latin extended
47Probability_of_default_according_to_AnaCreditDECIMALDOMReal numbers from 0 to 1 with 6 decimals
48StreetString (250) DOMString-ASCII up to 250 characters
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Party_identifierAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
6Institutional_unit_group_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each group.
7Ultimate_parent_undertaking_organisation_identifierThe party identifier of the ultimate parent undertaking accoring to Regulation (EU) 867/2016, defined as the "Counterparty identifier for the legal entity which is the ultimate parent undertaking of the counterparty. This ultimate parent undertaking has no parent undertaking. If the counterparty has no parent undertaking, the counterparty identifier for the counterparty itself is to be reported. Parent undertaking has the same meaning as defined in Article 4(1)(15)(a) of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.".
8Organisation_identifierThe party identifier of the involved organisation.
9Area_where_the_entity_is_located_according_to_SHSArea where the entity is located
10Controlled_by_national_or_foreign_bodiesControlled by national or foreign bodies specifies if a party is national private or foreign controlled according to European system of accounts (ESA) 2010.
11Country_codeThe country code according to ISO3166.
12Date_of_default_statusThe date the default status refers to.
13Default_statusA discriminator to distinguish between default because unlikely to pay, 90/180 days past due or both according to Regulation (EU) 575/2013, Article 178.
14Enterprise_indicatorEnterprise indicator specifies if a party is an enterprise according to EU recommendation 2003/361.
15Enterprise_sizeClassification of enterprises by size, in accordance with the Annex to Commission Recommendation 2003/361/EC.
16Enterprise_size_dateThe date to which the value provided in the ‘enterprise size’ refers. This is the date of the latest data used to classify or review the classification of the enterprise.
17Governed_Country_codeGoverned Country code is a code that indicates the country that is governed by the Central Government.
18Institutional_sectorInstitutional sectors in accordance with Regulation (EU) No 549/2013, Regulation (EU) No 575/2013 and Regulation (EU) No 1075/2013 of the European Central Bank (ECB/2013/40)(1).
20Leading_branch_for_AnaCredit_reporting_indicatorLeading branch indicator distinguishes Foreign branches into those that are considered leading branch for AnaCredit reporting and those that are Non-leading branches.
21Legal_formType of business entity as defined in the national legal system.
22Legal_proceeding_statusCategories describing an entity's legal status in relation to its/his/her solvency based on the national legal framework.
23Listed_central_bank_and_private_sector_company_indicatorAn indicator specifying is a security is listed or not.
24Market_capitalisation_indicatorMarket capitalisation indicator classifies the Listed central bank and private sectory company as Large or Small market capitalisation. 

Market capitalisation, or "market cap", of the Listed central bank and private sector company is the sum of the market capitalisations based on the market value of the total outstanding shares issued by the same listed legal entity or a group of legal entities across all stock markets globally, where the total outstanding shares issued by the group of legal entities refer to cases where the listed entity is a parent company of a group of legal entities.
Large market cap is defined as a market capitalisation equal to or greater than USD 2 billion and small market cap is defined as a market capitalisation of less than USD 2 billion.
(https://www.bis.org/basel_framework/chapter/MAR/21.htm?inforce=20230101&published=20200327)
25Multilateral_development_bank_indicatorMultilateral development bank indicator specifies if the International organisation is a multilateral development bank or not.
26Nomenclature_des_unités_territoriales_statistiques_(NUTS)_regionThe region inside a country according to NUTS.
27Organisation_type_by_legal_proceeding_statusA discriminator to distinguish between organisations where legal proceedings are ongoing and those without legal proceedings.
29Party_type_by_addressA discriminator to distinguish parties between different types of addresses.
30Performing_statusPerforming status in accordance with Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
31Performing_status_reasonThe reason for the choice of Performing status
32Pulling_effect_indicatorAn indicator specifying if the pulling effect is applied.
33Statistical_Classification_of_Economic_Activities_in_the_European_Community_(NACE)Classification of counterparties according to their economic activities, in accordance with the NACE revision 2 statistical classification as laid down in Regulation (EC) No 1893/2006 of the European Parliament and of the Council.
34Statistical_Classification_of_Economic_Activities_in_the_European_Community_revision_(NACE_2_1)Classification of counterparties according to their economic activities, in accordance with the NACE revision 2 statistical classification as laid down in Regulation (EC) No 1893/2006 of the European Parliament and of the Council.
35Type_of_economyThe economy to which the organisation relates (e.g. advanced or emerging economy)
37Annual_turnoverAnnual sales volume net of all discounts and sales taxes in accordance with Recommendation 2003/361/EC. Equivalent to the concept of 'total annual sales' in Article 153(4) of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.
38Balance_sheet_totalCarrying value of total assets in accordance with Regulation (EU) 549/2013.
39City_/_town_/_villageThe city, town or village.
40Date_of_initiation_of_legal_proceedingThe date on which the legal proceedings were initiated.
41First_nameThe first name.
42Last_nameThe last name.
43Legal_entity_identifier_(LEI)Reference code in line with the International Organization for Standardization’s (ISO) 17442 standard
44Number_of_employeesNumber of employees working for the counterparty, in accordance with Article 5 of the Annex to Recommendation 2003/361/EC.
45Organisation_nameThe organisation name.
46Postal_codeEntity's postal code.
47Probability_of_default_according_to_AnaCreditProbability of default according to AnaCredit.
The counterparty's probability of default over one year, determined in accordance with Articles 160, 163, 179 and 180 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013. 
48StreetEntity's street address, including the street number.

Default_status
Values List
ValueDescription
14Not in Default
18Default because both unlikely to pay and more than 90/180 days past due
19Default because unlikely to pay
20Default because more than 90/180 days past due
Enterprise_indicator
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Enterprise
2Not enterprise
Enterprise_size
Values List
ValueDescription
1Large enterprise: Enterprise not qualifying as a micro, small or medium-sized enterprise (SME), in accordance with the Annex to Recommendation 2003/361/EC.
2Medium enterprise: Enterprise qualifying as an SME, but not as a small or micro enterprise, in accordance with the Annex to Recommendation 2003/361/EC.
3Small enterprise: Enterprise qualifying as a small enterprise, in accordance with the Annex to Recommendation 2003/361/EC.
4Micro enterprise: Enterprise qualifying as a micro enterprise in accordance with the Annex to Recommendation 2003/361/EC.

Institutional_sector
Values List
ValueDescription
S1312State government (excluding social security funds): It corresponds to subsector S.1312, as defined in Regulation (EU) No 549/2013 (paragraph 2.115 of Annex A), extended to the rest of the world.
S1313Local government (excluding social security funds): It corresponds to subsector S.1313, as defined in Regulation (EU) No 549/2013 (paragraph 2.116 of Annex A), extended to the rest of the world.
