18Initial_exposure_class_of_the_securityYString (2) DOMExposure classes for the calculation of risk weight (Input Layer)
19Listed_indicatorYString (1) DOMListed indicator (Input Layer)
20Low_credit_risk_indicator_for_FINREPYString (1) DOMLow credit risk indicator for FINREP (Input Layer)
21Multiple_forbearance_measures_in_place_indicatorYString (1) DOMMultiple forbearance measures in place indicator (Input Layer)
22Negotiable_security_indicatorYString (1) DOMNegotiable security
23Non-performing_exit_criteria_met_indicatorYString (1) DOMNon-performing exit criteria met indicator (Input Layer)
24Option_type_(put/call)YString (1) DOMOption type (Input Layer)
25Performing_forborne_exposure_under_probation_reclassified_from_non-performing_indicatorYString (1) DOMPerforming forborne exposure under probation reclassified from non-performing indicator (Input Layer)
26Performing_status_reasonYString (1) DOMPerforming status reason
27Perpetual_debt_security_indicatorYString (2) DOMPerpetual debt security indicator (Input Layer)
28Primary_asset_classificationYString (6) DOMFull list of the classification of the instruments (Input Layer)
29RankYString (1) DOMFull list of the security rank level (Input Layer)
30Security_and_exchange_tradable_derivative_typeYString (2) DOMType of security or exchange tradable derivative(Input Layer)
31Security_guarantee_levelYString (1) DOMFull list of the Security Guarantee level (Input Layer)
32Security_levelYString (1) DOMFull list of the security level (Input Layer)
33Security_statusYString (3) DOMSecurity status (Input Layer)
34Security_type_by_identifierYString (1) DOMSecurity type by identifier (Input Layer)
35SeniorityYString (1) DOMSeniority (Input Layer)
36Significant_asset_classYString (1) DOMSignificant asset class (Input Layer)
37Structured_note_indicatorYString (1) DOMStructured note indicator (Input Layer)
38Subordinated_debt_indicatorYString (1) DOMSubordinated debt indicator (Input Layer)
39Type_of_asset_provided_as_securityYString (5) DOMType of asset provided as security (Input Layer)
40Liability_encumbrance_residual_maturity_bandYStringDOMResidual maturity band (Input Layer)
41Date_/_period_of_application_of_forbearance_measure_typeYString (2) DOMDate / period of application of forbearance measure
42Time_past_due_bandYStringDOMTime past due band (Input Layer)
43Past_due_debt_security_indicatorYString (2) DOMPast due debt security indicator (Input layer)
44Renegotiated_debt_security_typeYStringDOMRenegotiated debt security type (Input Layer)
45Accumulated_recoveries_since_defaultDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
46Date_of_forbearance_statusDateDOMAll possible dates
47Date_of_performing_statusDateDOMAll possible dates
48Date_of_renegotiation_statusDateDOMAll possible dates
49Expiration_dateDateDOMAll possible dates
50Forbearance_measure_countIntegerDOMInteger
51International_securities_identification_numberString (12) DOMString up to 12 characters - ISIN
52Issue_dateDateDOMAll possible dates
53Legal_final_maturity_dateDateDOMAll possible dates
54Official_security_identifierString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
55Original_nominal_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
56Date_past_dueDateDOMAll possible dates
57Security_nameString (250) DOMString-ASCII up to 250 characters
58Security_status_dateDateDOMAll possible dates
59Start_dateDateDOMAll possible dates
60Total_amount_of_capital_issuedDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
61Total_number_of_shares_issuedDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
62Exchange_tradable_derivative_type_by_identifierString (1) DOMExchange tradable derivative type by identifier (Input Layer)
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Security_and_exchange_tradable_derivative_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
6(Extremely)_high_liquidity_and_credit_quality_indicatorAn indicator specifying if an asset or collateral received is considered extremely high liquidity and credit quality (HQLA) eligible according to Regulation (EU) 680/2014, Annex 17 (Asset encumbrance), part 2, 2.1.2, column 035.
7Accrued_interests_for_market_values_indicatorAn indicator specifying if accrued interest for market values are included or not. Further details may be found in the Guidance notes to reporting agents on SHS regulation (see https://www.ecb.europa.eu/pub/pdf/other/guidance_notes_to_reporting_agents_on_shs_regulation201705.en.pdf).
8Collateral_geographical_location_for_SHSGeographical allocation of the underlying assets as defined by the SHS regulation.
9CurrencyThe currency code according to ISO4217.
10Currency_of_transaction_reportedCurrency of transaction reported
11Debt_security_by_accounting_standardDebt security by accounting standard classifies the Debt security according to the specific applicability of its figures for either IFRS or nGAAP.
12Debt_security_by_Performing_status_typePerforming status in accordance with Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
13Early_redemptions_included_indicatorAn indicator specifying if early redemptions have been included.
14Eligible_for_central_bank_funding_indicatorSpecifies if an object is eligible for central bank funding in the sense that it might be exchanged / provided as collateral for funding.
15Forbearance_measure_typeA discriminator to distinguish between refinanced debt, instruments with modified interest rate below market value and other modified terms.
16Geographical_location_of_collateralThe country code of the location of the collateral according to ISO3166.
17Identifier_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different  identifier types for securities.
18Initial_exposure_class_of_the_securityThe exposure class, according to Regulation (EU) 575/2013, Article 112, that the security would be assigned to without applying any credit risk mitigation techniques.
19Listed_indicatorAn indicator specifying is a security is listed or not.
20Low_credit_risk_indicator_for_FINREPLow credit risk indicator for FINREP signals if a debt security or loan or advance has low credit risk or not, according to the Banks' internal assessments.
21Multiple_forbearance_measures_in_place_indicatorForbearance measures were granted in addition to already existing forbearance measures. It concerns forborne exposures under probation to which forbearance measures were applied in addition to forbearance measures granted at an earlier point in time, without the exposure having cured in between.
22Negotiable_security_indicatorNegotiable security indicator indicates whether or not the ownership of the International securities identification number security is readily capable of being transferred from one unit to another by delivery or endorsement or of being offset in the case of financial derivatives.

While any security can be potentially traded, negotiable securties are designed to be traded on an organised exchange or over-the-counter, although actual trading is not a necessary condition for negotiability.
An instrument that does not fulfill these conditions is meant to be ‘non-negotiable’.
23Non-performing_exit_criteria_met_indicatorNon-performing forborne exposures that failed to meet the non-performing exit criteria.
24Option_type_(put/call)Option type (put/call) indicates if the Exchange tradable option is a put option or a call option.
25Performing_forborne_exposure_under_probation_reclassified_from_non-performing_indicatorAn indicator specifying if an asset is classified as Non-performing forborne exposure under probation reclassified from non-performing or not according to Regulation (EU) 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.260.
26Performing_status_reasonThe reason for the choice of Performing status
27Perpetual_debt_security_indicatorPerpetual debt security indicator classifies debt security into those that do not have a maturity date and are therefor a Perpetual debt security, and those that do have a maturity date and are thus a Non-perpetual debty security
28Primary_asset_classificationTruncated primary asset classification
29RankA debt security's rank, like junior, mezanine or senior.
31Security_guarantee_levelIt defines the security ‘guarantee level’  whether the instrument is guaranteed by an entity different from the issuer, e.g. by the government. Further information may be found in the Guidance notes to reporting agents on SHS regulation (see https://www.ecb.europa.eu/pub/pdf/other/guidance_notes_to_reporting_agents_on_shs_regulation201705.en.pdf).
32Security_levelIt defines the ‘security level’ whether an instrument is secured or unsecured, i.e. whether there is recourse to the cash flows of a securitised portfolio.
33Security_statusIndicates the status of the security, i.e. whether the instrument is alive (active) or not alive (not active).
34Security_type_by_identifierA discriminator to distinguish between different securities by identifier, specifically ISIN and non-ISIN securities.
35SeniorityThe seniority of the security, e.g. junior, mezzanine, senior.
36Significant_asset_classSignificant asset class distinguishes Exchange tradable derivative into specific asset classes, like interest rate, equity, foreign exchange.
37Structured_note_indicatorAn indicator specifying if a security is a structured note.
38Subordinated_debt_indicatorAn indicator specifying if an asset is considered as subordinated debt or not.
39Type_of_asset_provided_as_securitySpecifies the type of asset backed security or covered bond, e.g. mortgage backed securities.
41Date_/_period_of_application_of_forbearance_measure_typeDate / period of application of forbearance measure type classifies if the forbearance measure date is applicable to the current reporting period or not.
42Time_past_due_bandA Time past due band is according to the EBA Annex 5 for Financial Reporting (FinRep) classifying Non-performing exposures (NPEs), exposures that represent both past due amounts and a likelihood of non-full repayment. These exposures shall be allocated to their respective bracket according to the ammount of days that they have been unpaid or overdue.
43Past_due_debt_security_indicatorPast due debt security indicator classifies debt security into those that are currently performing, and those that are past due in accordance with Part 2.48 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
45Accumulated_recoveries_since_defaultThe total amount recovered from the Debt security since the date of default.
46Date_of_forbearance_statusThe date on which forbearance measure have been applied.
47Date_of_performing_statusThe date on which the performing status is considered to have been established or changed.
48Date_of_renegotiation_statusThe date on which the renegotiation status is considered to have been established or changed.
49Expiration_dateThe Expiration date of an option is a date that is the last day on which the option holder can exercise their right to buy or sell the underlying asset. 
50Forbearance_measure_countNumber of times an exposure has been under forbearance measures. Exposures classified as forborne at the reporting reference date, to which forbearance measures have been applied at two, respectively more than two different points in time. That includes, among others, originally forborne exposures that exited the forborne status (cured forborne exposures), but were granted new forbearance measures after that.
51International_securities_identification_numberISIN of the security.
52Issue_dateThe date on which the securities are delivered to the underwriter by the issuer against payment. This is the date when the securities are available for delivery to investors for the first time. Note: For a strip this column indicates the date on which the
53Legal_final_maturity_dateThe contractual maturity date of the asset or liability, taking into account any agreements amending initial contracts.
54Official_security_identifierOfficial security identifier.
55Original_nominal_amountThe original nominal amount. E.g. for debt securities issued it is the nominal amount at issuance date.
56Date_past_dueThe date on which the asset became past due in accordance with Part 2.48 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014. This is the latest such date prior to the reporting reference date and it is to be reported if the instrument is past due on the reporting reference date.
57Security_nameThe name of the security.
58Security_status_dateIndicates the Date at which the Security Status has changed from alive to not-alive
59Start_dateStart date is a date that indicates when the Exchange tradable derivative goes into effect.
60Total_amount_of_capital_issuedIt is the face value of the shares that have been issued to the shareholders.
61Total_number_of_shares_issuedThe total number of shares issued.

Security_and_exchange_tradable_derivative_identifier

 
Table NameSecurity_borrowing_and_lending_transaction_including_cash_as_collateral_component
Functional NameSecurity borrowing and lending transaction including cash as collateral component
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Securities borrowing and lending transaction including cash as collateral component is a component of a Securities borrowing and lending transaction, i.e. a Security borrowed, a Security lent or cash as collateral.
Notes
Number Of Columns10
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Accounting_standardPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
5Instrument_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6CurrencyYString (3) DOMSubdomain for domain ISO-4217 Currency Codes (ISO4217 Domain) (Input Layer)
7Securities_borrowing_and_lending_transaction_including_cash_as_collateral_component_typeYString (1) DOMType of specific securities borrowing and lending transaction including cash as collateral component (Input Layer)
8Security_borrowing_and_lending_transaction_component_type_by_directionYString (2) DOMSecurity borrowing and lending transaction component type by direction (Input layer)
9Nominal_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
10Number_of_sharesDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Instrument_identifierAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
6CurrencyThe currency code according to ISO4217.
8Security_borrowing_and_lending_transaction_component_type_by_directionA discriminator to distinguish between different types of security borrowing and lending transaction components by direction.
9Nominal_amountHolding amount at nominal value
10Number_of_sharesThe number of shares of a security.
Indexes

Table NameSecurity_borrowing_component_Security_Collateral_assignment
Functional NameSecurity borrowing component Security Collateral assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionSecurity borrowing component Security Collateral assignment is the combination of the Security borrowing component and the Security collateral that indicates which Security collateral has been borrowed via the Security borrowing component.
Notes
Number Of Columns6
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Security_collateral_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2Reference_datePFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYStringDOMSecurity position Balance sheet recognised type (Input Layer)
5Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
6Instrument_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Security_collateral_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each collateral.
2Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
3Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
4Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
6Instrument_identifierAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
Indexes
Index NameStateFunctionalSpatialExpressionColumn NameSort
Order

Table NameSecurity_position
Functional NameSecurity position
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Security position is the combination of a Security and an Investor that indicates which Investor has invested in which Security.
Notes
Number Of Columns9
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Investor_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
2Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
3Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
5Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
6Security_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Investor_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8Security_or_Exchange_tradable_derivative_position_typeYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
9Own_company_investment_indicatorYString (1) DOMOwn company investment indicator (Input Layer)
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Investor_role_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
2Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
3Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
4Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
6Security_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
7Investor_identifierA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
8Security_or_Exchange_tradable_derivative_position_typeA discriminator to distinguish between security positions and tradable derivative positions.
9Own_company_investment_indicatorOwn company investment indicator is an indicator to Security position derived data, which determines if the investment in the Security position is in a subsidiary, joint venture or associate of the own company or not
Indexes
Index NameStateFunctionalSpatialExpressionColumn NameSort
Order

Table NameSecurity_position_hedged_by_Exchange_tradable_derivative_position
Functional NameSecurity position hedged by Exchange tradable derivative position
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionSecurity position hedged by Exchange tradable derivative position is the combination of a Long/short balance sheet recognised security position and an Exchange tradable derivative position as a hedge that indicates which (portion of a) Security position is hedged by which Exchange tradable derivative position as a hedge.
Notes
Number Of Columns15
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
4Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
5Seller_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
6Seller_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore

11Investor_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
12Security_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
13Long/Short_balance_sheet_recognised_security_position_typePFYStringDOMLong/Short balance sheet recognised security position type (Input Layer)
14Exchange_tradable_derivative_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
15Exchange_tradable_derivative_position_role_typePFYString (2) DOMExchange tradable derivative position role type (Input Layer)
Columns Comments

Table NameSecurity_position_hedged_by_Over_the_counter_(OTC)_derivative
Functional NameSecurity position hedged by Over the counter (OTC) derivative
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Security position hedged by Over the counter (OTC) derivative is the combination of a Long/Short balance sheet recognised security position and an Over the counter (OTC) derivative as a hedge that indicates which (portion of a Long/short balance sheet recognised security position is hedged by which Over the counter (OTC) derivative (acting in the role of an Over the counter (OTC) derivative as a hedge).
Notes
Number Of Columns13
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation

2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
5Instrument_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore

NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Instrument_identifierAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
7Investor_identifierAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
8Investor_role_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
9Security_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
11Security_position_hedged_by_Over_the_counter_(OTC)_derivative_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different types of Security positions hedged by Over the counter (OTC) derivative.
12Nominal_amountHolding amount at nominal value
13Number_of_sharesThe number of shares of a security.

Table NameSignificant_currency_10%_depreciation_contingent_encumbrance
Functional NameSignificant currency 10% depreciation contingent encumbrance
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionContingent encumbrance refers to the additional assets which may need to be encumbered when the reporting institutions faces adverse developments triggered by an external event over which the reporting institution has no control (including a downgrade, decrease of the fair value of the encumbered assets or a general loss of confidence). In these cases, the reporting institution will need to encumber additional assets as a consequence of already existing transactions. The additional amount of encumbered assets shall be net of the impact of the institution’s hedge transactions against the events described under the aforementioned stressed scenarios. (Annext 17 (Asset Encumbrance) 4. Part C)
Notes
Number Of Columns18
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level
4Accounting_standardPYString (1) DOMAccounting framework
5Significant_currencyPYString (3) DOMSubdomain for domain ISO-4217 Currency Codes (ISO4217 Domain)
6Collateralised_deposits_other_than_repurchase_agreements_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
7Collateralised_deposits_other_than_repurchase_agreements_with_central_banks_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
8Debt_securities_issued_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
9Debt_securities_issued_as_asset-backed_securities_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
10Debt_securities_issued_as_covered_bonds_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
11Deposits_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
12Derivatives_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals

14Over-The-Counter_(OTC)_derivatives_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals

Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Significant_currencySignificant currency is that currency that the reporting agent has deemed significant for the purpose of reporting Asset Encubrance Contingent scenario 'Significant currency depreciation'. It contains the currency code according to ISO4217.
6Collateralised_deposits_other_than_repurchase_agreements_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountCollateralised deposits other than repurchase agreements 10% currency depreciation carrying amount in Scenario B is a simulation of a 10% depreciation in each currency in which the institution has aggregate Collateralised deposits other than repurchase agreement liabilities amounting to or exceeding 5% of the institution’s total liabilities.
7Collateralised_deposits_other_than_repurchase_agreements_with_central_banks_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountCollateralised deposits other than repurchase agreements with central banks 10% currency depreciation carrying amount in Scenario B is a simulation of a 10% depreciation in each currency in which the institution has aggregate Collateralised deposits other than repurchase agreement liabilities amounting to or exceeding 5% of the institution’s total liabilities.
8Debt_securities_issued_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountDebt securities issued 10% currency depreciation carrying amount in Scenario B is a simulation of a 10% depreciation in each currency in which the institution has aggregate Debt securities issued liabilities amounting to or exceeding 5% of the institution’s total liabilities.
9Debt_securities_issued_as_asset-backed_securities_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountDebt securities issued as asset-backed securities issued 10% currency depreciation carrying amount in Scenario B is a simulation of a 10% depreciation in each currency in which the institution has aggregate Debt securities issued as asset-backed securities liabilities amounting to or exceeding 5% of the institution’s total liabilities.
10Debt_securities_issued_as_covered_bonds_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountDebt securities issued as covered bonds issued 10% currency depreciation carrying amount in Scenario B is a simulation of a 10% depreciation in each currency in which the institution has aggregate Debt securities issued as covered bond Debt securities issued as covered bonds issued liabilities amounting to or exceeding 5% of the institution’s total liabilities.
11Deposits_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountDeposits 10% currency depreciation carrying amount in Scenario B is a simulation of a 10% depreciation in each currency in which the institution has aggregate deposit liabilities amounting to or exceeding 5% of the institution’s total liabilities.
12Derivatives_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountDerivatives 10% currency depreciation carrying amount in Scenario B is a simulation of a 10% depreciation in each currency in which the institution has aggregate derivative liabilities amounting to or exceeding 5% of the institution’s total liabilities.
13Other_sources_of_encumbrance_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountOther sources of encumbrance 10% currency depreciation carrying amount in Scenario B is a simulation of a 10% depreciation in each currency in which the institution has aggregate liabilities that are other sources of encumbrance amounting to or exceeding 5% of the institution’s total liabilities.
14Over-The-Counter_(OTC)_derivatives_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountOver-The-Counter (OTC) derivatives 10% currency depreciation carrying amount in Scenario B is a simulation of a 10% depreciation in each currency in which the institution has aggregate Over-The-Counter (OTC) derivative liabilities amounting to or exceeding 5% of the institution’s total liabilities.
15Repurchase_agreements_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountRepurchase agreements 10% currency depreciation carrying amount in Scenario B is a simulation of a 10% depreciation in each currency in which the institution has aggregate repurchase agreements liabilities amounting to or exceeding 5% of the institution’s total liabilities.
16Repurchase_agreements_with_central_banks_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountRepurchase agreements with central banks 10% currency depreciation carrying amount in Scenario B is a simulation of a 10% depreciation in each currency in which the institution has aggregate Repurchase agreements with central bank liabilities amounting to or exceeding 5% of the institution’s total liabilities.
17Selected_financial_liablities_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountSelected financial liablities 10% currency depreciation carrying amount in Scenario B is a simulation of a 10% depreciation in each currency in which the institution has aggregate liabilities amounting to or exceeding 5% of the institution’s total liabilities.
18Total_sources_of_encumbrance_%_currency_depreciation_carrying_amountTotal sources of encumbrance 10% currency depreciation carrying amount in Scenario B is a simulation of a 10% depreciation in each currency in which the institution has aggregate liabilities amounting to or exceeding 5% of the institution’s total liabilities.

Table NameSubsidiary,_joint_venture_and_associate_Other_organisation_role_assignment
Functional NameSubsidiary, joint venture and associate Other organisation role assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Subsidiary, joint venture and associate Central bank and private sector company role assignment is the combination of a Subsidiary, joint venture and associate and an Central bank and private sector company role that indicates which Subsidiary, joint venture and associate comprises which Central bank and private sector company role.
Notes
Number Of Columns17
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
2Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
3Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
4Other_organisation_role_typePFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
5Party_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Group_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8Entry_dateYDateDOMAll possible dates
9Accounting_treatment_(IFRS_Group)YString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
10Accounting_treatment_(CRR_Group)YString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
11Accumulated_equity_interest_(%)YDECIMALDOMReal numbers from 0 to 1 with 6 decimals
12Acquisition_costYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
13Carrying_amountYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
14Fair_value_of_investments_for_which_there_are_published_price_quotationsYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
15Goodwill_link_to_the_investeeYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
16Subsidiary,_joint_venture_and_associate_Other_organisation_assignment_typeYString (1) DOMSubsidiary, joint venture and associate Other organisation role assignment
17Voting_rights_(%)YDECIMALDOMReal numbers from 0 to 1 with 6 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
2Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
4Other_organisation_role_typeThe organisation role.
5Party_identifierThe party identifier of the involved organisation.
6Group_identifierThe group identifier of the involved subsidiary, joint venture and associate.
7Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
8Entry_dateThe date in which the investee entered within the ‘scope of the group’ Annex V 296
9Accounting_treatment_(IFRS_Group)The accounting treatment of the subsidiary, joint venture or associate with respect to the IFRS group.
10Accounting_treatment_(CRR_Group)The accounting treatment of the subsidiary, joint venture or associate with respect to the CRR group.
11Accumulated_equity_interest_(%)Accumulated equity interest (%)’ shall be the percentage of ownership instruments held by the institution as of the reference date FinRep Annex V 296.
12Acquisition_costThe amount paid by the investors  FinRep Annex V 296.
13Carrying_amountThe carrying amount in accordance with Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
14Fair_value_of_investments_for_which_there_are_published_price_quotationsMeans the price at the reference date; it shall be provided only if the instruments are quoted.Annex V 296.
15Goodwill_link_to_the_investeeThe amount of goodwill reported on the consolidated balance sheet of the reporting institution for the investee in the items ‘goodwill’ or ‘investments in subsidiaries, joint ventures and associated’ FinRep Annex V 296.
16Subsidiary,_joint_venture_and_associate_Other_organisation_assignment_typeThe subsidiary, joint venture and associate type.
17Voting_rights_(%)Voting rights in percent.

Table NameSyndicated_contract
Functional NameSyndicated contract
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionSyndicated contract is the overall contract of the syndication in the administration of the lead arranger of the syndicated contract.
Notes
Number Of Columns1
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Syndicated_contract_identifierPYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Syndicated_contract_identifier 'Contract identifier' applied by the lead arranger of the syndicated contract to uniquely identify each contract. Each syndicated contract will have one 'syndicated contract identifier'. This value will not change over time and cannot be used by the lead arranger as the contract identifier for any other contract. All creditors participating in the syndicated contract must use the same 'syndicated contract identifier'.

Table NameSynthetic_securitisation
Functional NameSynthetic securitisation
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionRegulation (EU) 2402/2017, Article 2(10):
‘Synthetic securitisation’ means a securitisation where the transfer of risk is achieved by the use of credit derivatives or guarantees, and the exposures being securitised remain exposures of the originator.
Notes
Number Of Columns14
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
4Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
5Securitisation_identifierPYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6Asset_pool_identifierFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Capital_purposes_derecognition_amountYDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
8Percentage_retainedYDECIMALDOMReal numbers from 0 to 1 with 6 decimals
9Re-securitisation_indicatorYString (1) DOMRe-securitisation indicator
10Simple,_transparent_and_standardised_(STS)_securitisation_indicatorYString (1) DOMSimple, transparent and standardised (STS) securitisation indicator
11Significant_risk_transfer_indicatorYString (1) DOMSignificant risk transfer indicator
12Securitisation_and_other_credit_transfer_typeYString (1) DOMSecuritisation and other credit transfer type
13Securitisation_typeYString (1) DOMSecuritisation type
14Synthetic_securitisation_typeYString (1) DOMSynthetic securitisation type
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Securitisation_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
6Asset_pool_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each asset pool.
7Capital_purposes_derecognition_amountCapital purposes derecognition amount is an amount for significant risk transfer securitisation that shall include the carrying amount of the financial assets recognised for accounting purposes but de-recognised for prudential purposes because the institution is treating them as securitisation positions for capital purposes in accordance with Articles 109, 243 and 244 CRR.

It can be used to report the amount derecognised for capital purposes in FINREP.
8Percentage_retainedThe percentage that has been retained.
9Re-securitisation_indicatorAn indicator specifying if a securitisation is considered a re-securitisation.
10Simple,_transparent_and_standardised_(STS)_securitisation_indicatorAn indicator specifying if a securitisation is considered a simple, transparent and standardised (STS) securitisation acccording to Regulation (EU) 2402/2017, Article 19.
11Significant_risk_transfer_indicatorAn indicator specifying if a securitisation establishes a significant risk transfer according to Regulation (EU) 575/2013, Article 243 (2) and Article 244 (2).
12Securitisation_and_other_credit_transfer_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different types of securitisations and other credit transfer, like securitisation and covered bond programs.
13Securitisation_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different types of securitisations like traditional and sysnthetic securitisation.
14Synthetic_securitisation_typeA discriminator to distinguish between synthetic securitisations with and without involvement of securitisation special purpose entities (SSPEs).
Indexes

Table NameTraditional_securitisation
Functional NameDiscount or excess spread
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionRegulation (EU) 2402/2017, Article 2 (9):
´Traditional securitisation’ means a securitisation involving the transfer of the economic interest in the exposures being securitised through the transfer of ownership of those exposures from the originator to an SSPE or through sub-participation by an SSPE, where the securities issued do not represent payment obligations of the originator. 
Notes
Number Of Columns15
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear

7Asset_pool_identifierFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore

10Re-securitisation_indicatorYString (1) DOMRe-securitisation indicator
11Simple,_transparent_and_standardised_(STS)_securitisation_indicatorYString (1) DOMSimple, transparent and standardised (STS) securitisation indicator
12Significant_risk_transfer_indicatorYString (1) DOMSignificant risk transfer indicator
13Securitisation_and_other_credit_transfer_typeYString (1) DOMSecuritisation and other credit transfer type
14Securitisation_typeYString (1) DOMSecuritisation type
15Discount_or_excess_spread_amountDECIMALDOMNon-negative real numbers with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
2Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5Securitisation_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
6Credit_facility_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each credit facility.
7Asset_pool_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each asset pool.
8Capital_purposes_derecognition_amountCapital purposes derecognition amount is an amount for significant risk transfer securitisation that shall include the carrying amount of the financial assets recognised for accounting purposes but de-recognised for prudential purposes because the institution is treating them as securitisation positions for capital purposes in accordance with Articles 109, 243 and 244 CRR.

It can be used to report the amount derecognised for capital purposes in FINREP.
9Percentage_retainedThe percentage that has been retained.
10Re-securitisation_indicatorAn indicator specifying if a securitisation is considered a re-securitisation.
11Simple,_transparent_and_standardised_(STS)_securitisation_indicatorAn indicator specifying if a securitisation is considered a simple, transparent and standardised (STS) securitisation acccording to Regulation (EU) 2402/2017, Article 19.
12Significant_risk_transfer_indicatorAn indicator specifying if a securitisation establishes a significant risk transfer according to Regulation (EU) 575/2013, Article 243 (2) and Article 244 (2).
13Securitisation_and_other_credit_transfer_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different types of securitisations and other credit transfer, like securitisation and covered bond programs.
14Securitisation_typeA discriminator to distinguish between different types of securitisations like traditional and sysnthetic securitisation.
15Discount_or_excess_spread_amountThe amount of discount in a traditional securitisation.
Indexes

Order

Table NameTranche_in_a_Traditional_securitisation
Functional NameTranche in a Traditional securitisation
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionRegulation (EU) 2402/2017, Article 2, (6):
´Tranche’ means a contractually established segment of the credit risk associated with an exposure or a pool of exposures, where a position in the segment entails a risk of credit loss greater than or less than a position of the same amount in another segment, without taking account of credit protection provided by third parties directly to the holders of positions in the segment or in other segments.
Notes
Number Of Columns9
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Enriched Input Layer with Full Names 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1Securitisation_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2Reference_datePFYDateDOMAll possible dates
3Reporting_agent_identifierPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4Accounting_consolidation_levelPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
5Accounting_standardPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
6Asset_backed_security_identifierPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7Securitisation_tranche_typeYString (1) DOMSecuritisation tranche type
8Tranche_identifierString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
9Tranche_nameString (250) DOMString-ASCII up to 250 characters
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1Securitisation_identifierAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
2Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
3Reporting_agent_identifierIdentifier of the reporting agent.
4Accounting_consolidation_levelAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
6Asset_backed_security_identifierThe security identifier of the involved asset backed security.
7Securitisation_tranche_typeType of securitisation tranche distinguishes Securitisation tranche according to the type of securitisation that is used to tranche the pool.
8Tranche_identifierTranche identifier is a number that uniquely identifies the Tranche.
9Tranche_nameTranche name is a free-format text that identifies how the tranche is colloquially known.

Table NameTranche_in_a_synthetic_securitisation_without_securitisation_special_purpose_entity_(SSPE)_being_a_deposit
Functional NameTranche in a synthetic securitisation without securitisation special purpose entity (SSPE) being a deposit
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Tranche in a synthetic securitisation without securitisation special purpose entity (SSPE) being a Deposit is a Tranche in a synthetic securitisation without securitisation special purpose entity (SSPE) that is a Deposit (acting as a collateral).
Notes
Number Of Columns10
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear

kind		Domain NameFormula

NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes

5Reference_dateThe date the data refers to.
